Yoga does not end when the āsana practice is over. In many ways, it is just beginning. We cultivate strength so that we may hold peace as a living prayer, even, and especially, in times of chaos and uncertainty.

The practice traditionally closes with the ritual chanting of Om Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ, repeated three times in reference to the tāpa-traya, the three kinds of suffering. The first invocation is for peace within, a quieting of inner turbulence, a settling of the mind and heart. The second is for peace in the external world, a sincere wish for harmony among all beings, a hope that understanding may prevail over conflict. The third is for peace in the unseen, cosmic realm, where the subtle currents of life move beyond personal control.

Ending the practice with this chant reminds us that peace begins within, radiates outward, and ultimately rests in the vastness that holds us all.

Meet @demariayogadance 

One of Ibiza’s most respected yoga teachers, James has been sharing his practice with adults and children for over two decades.

Before discovering yoga, James trained as a professional dancer in London—first at the Central School of Ballet, and then at the London Contemporary Dance School, where he developed a deep understanding of release technique under teachers like Larren Potter, Gill Clarke and Paul Douglas. He also studied Pilates with Alan Herdman, adding another layer of awareness to his movement practice.

James brings this background into his teaching, weaving together dance, Pilates and release work to create a flowing style that feels both grounded and free. His classes invite you to connect with your breath, explore postures in a way that suits your body, and experience the natural rhythm of movement.

You’ll also explore breathing techniques and Mudras to encourage the flow of prana (life force), and take time to rest and restore with relaxation and meditation.

Whether you’re completely new to yoga or returning to deepen your practice, James creates a space that’s welcoming, personal, and tuned in to how you feel in the moment.

What are the Bandhas?

In Sanskrit, bandha comes from the root bandh — to bind, to hold, to gather. They are often called “locks,” but more than physical contractions, they are gateways that channel prāṇa, the vital force, toward awakening.

There are three main bandhas:

Mūla Bandha — the root lock (mūla = root), a subtle lift at the pelvic floor that steadies energy at its source.

Uḍḍīyāna Bandha — the flying upward lock (ud = upward, ḍī = to fly), an abdominal lift after exhalation that sends energy soaring like a bird in flight.

 Jālandhara Bandha — the throat lock (jāla = net, dhara = holding), a gentle lowering of the chin that creates a subtle net guiding prāṇan inward.

When practiced together, they form Mahā Bandha — the Great Lock.

At first the bandhas feel physical, but over time they reveal themselves as energetic openings, guiding practice from the outer body into the subtle realm. Always learn them gently, and under the guidance of a skilled teacher. Ultimately, the bandhas are less about “locking” than about releasing—freeing the practitioner into the vast, flowing energy of yoga.

Recently someone asked me: “ what’s the point of doing these fancy poses? Is it just to show off?”

Well…for some-possibly. But that doesn’t change the fact that they had to spend lots and lots of hours practicing to get to a place to do these poses. 

There’s a way deeper reason for it though. 

My Iyengar teacher once told me : “ in yoga-every pose is magic”. 

And that simple sentence made me understand the purpose of asanas (poses) in a whole new level. 

Each pose works with different physical, mental and  energetic aspects of us. Learning more about”advanced” poses is a humbling journey. Building strength, trust and confidence. 

I’ve been practicing asanas for 10 years, nearly every day. There is still more poses that i CAN’T do than the ones I can. And most of them i’ll never be able to do because my bone structure simply doesn’t allow me. 

Yet still I keep 1 or 2 poses that i’m working towards, regularly. Because the attempt to be better, is what makes me better.

▫️HANDSTAND ALIGNMENT DRILLS▫️

Improve your line with these simple, effective drills 👇

1️⃣ Prone lift — Lie on your belly with a block under your upper thighs and wrists. Lift your belly off the floor, draw your ribs in, and gently round through the upper back. Activate your core!

2️⃣ Supine lift — Lie on your back with a block under your calves and upper back. Reach your arms overhead to the floor, lift your belly and back up, and engage your core to find that hollow shape.

3️⃣ Elastic tension drill — Stand with a thin elastic band under your feet and looped around your hands. As you raise your arms overhead, pull the belly in and gently tuck the pelvis to stack your line.

4️⃣ Straddle push — Place a small Pilates band around your big toes, hop into a mini straddle handstand, and focus on strong shoulder push and tension through the band.

✨ These drills help you connect your core, shoulders, and hips for a straighter, more controlled handstand line.

In a world obsessed with finding the next nervous system regulation tool to finally fix anxiety, I’ve been noticing something that feels both obvious and surprisingly difficult:

Some of my calmest moments don’t come from doing more. They come from being where I already am.

And I say this as someone who constantly catches herself reaching for the next thing too.

The next podcast.
The next insight.
The next healing tool.
The next thing that will finally make me feel more grounded, more regulated, more “there.”

But lately I’ve been noticing how much changes when I slow down enough to actually be in the moment I’m in.

Drinking my coffee without my phone.
Walking without filling the silence.
Cooking without rushing to finish.

Sometimes you don’t need more wellness tips, you just need to slow down enough to be able to listen to that inner wisdom you already have.

Beautiful Bali 🌺 I can hardly believe that the first time I visited this island was nearly twenty years ago. So much has changed, and yet so much has remained the same, both within myself and here on the island.

It feels like a blessing to be here for the next two weeks, immersed in sharing the practice with everyone who will join. Together we will intentionally create space for deep practice, entering a kind of bubble where introspection becomes possible and where transformation can quietly unfold.

With everything happening in the world right now, I am deeply aware of what a privilege it is simply to be safe and to have the opportunity to practice and teach yoga. It is not something I take for granted. Even if it may seem a little naive, I still believe that when we practice sincerely, we participate in something larger than ourselves. Through small acts of presence, discipline, and compassion, yoga gives us a way to slowly, patiently help change the world.

💕

This quote is a beautiful expression of Metta (loving-kindness or goodwill), a core teaching in Buddhism. It comes from the Karaniya Metta Sutta: The Discourse on Loving-kindness, which is the Buddha’s guide on how to cultivate boundless, unconditional love. 

The passage teaches that you should wish unconditional peace, joy, and goodwill toward every single living thing in existence. It is an exercise in expanding the heart to exclude absolutely no one..

May all beings be peaceful.
May all beings be happy.

Sometimes the things we label as “just my personality” are actually patterns our nervous system learned to survive, not to thrive. If you see yourself in any of these, it’s not a personal failure - it’s information about what your body might have been trying to protect you from.

🌀Feeling anxious when things are too quiet or peaceful
🌀Apologizing automatically — even when nothing is your fault
🌀Needing constant background noise to feel “okay”
🌀Over-explaining yourself in normal conversations
🌀Panicking when someone says “we need to talk” (even casually)
🌀Feeling guilty when you rest or do nothing productive
🌀Scanning rooms and people’s moods the second you walk in
🌀Struggling to identify what you’re actually feeling (just “bad” or “off”)
🌀Getting uncomfortable when someone is genuinely kind to you
🌀Feeling more relaxed in chaos than in stability

Be kind. You never really know what is going on in another person’s life.

We are all souls navigating this human experience…

When someone steps onto their mat, they might be carrying so much more than we will ever see.

Someone arriving with quiet grief
someone holding joy they haven’t shared yet
someone rebuilding their strength
someone processing change
someone learning how to soften again
someone overwhelmed but trying
someone celebrating a personal win
someone holding exhaustion in their bones

This is why tenderness matters.
Why presence matters.
Why the way we hold space matters.

Because every person on the mat is carrying a whole human story…

and the practice becomes a place where they can land
breathe
feel
and come home to themselves
at their own pace.

The mat doesn’t judge, our circles, souls holding space for each other, don’t judge. It’s like a warm cocoon where you can let your guard down, surrender and really feel into all of the emotions that rise and fall inside of you.

It is safe to let yourself feel because you are not alone🤍🥹

Assisting in yoga opens a channel of communication that reaches beyond the rational mind. When a teacher offers a hands-on assist, the dialogue is not primarily verbal or conceptual. It unfolds through sensation, breath, timing, and presence. There is a kind of kinesthetic intelligence that moves through the contact, a subtle exchange in which the body often understands something before the intellect can name it. A well-timed touch can invite the spine to lengthen, help the breath soften, or reveal a pathway of support that the student may not have felt on their own. In this way, assisting has long been part of the embodied pedagogy of traditions like Ashtanga yoga, where learning is transmitted not only through explanation but through lived, physical experience.

We remain sincerely thankful to each person who practices with us and helps cultivate a space where the teachings of yoga can continue to unfold with integrity, compassion, and care.

💕

Why practice āsana? It is a question that every practitioner must eventually ask themselves, because the answer becomes the ground upon which the entire path rests. Without a clear sense of purpose, practice can easily drift toward performance, ambition, or habit. Yet when we return to the deeper intention behind āsana, the practice begins to reveal its true function. It becomes a doorway into the interior life.

The purpose of āsana practice is not simply flexibility, strength, or even physical health, although these are welcome benefits. Its deeper purpose is to help us rediscover the sacred landscape within ourselves, the place where presence is steady, where compassion naturally arises, and where the quiet intelligence of life reveals itself. When we practice with this understanding, every posture becomes more than an exercise. It becomes an exploration of consciousness itself.

Do I want to fit in, or do I want to belong?
It’s a question I keep coming back to.

Fitting in asks you to shrink a little. 
To smooth your edges.

Belonging asks for the opposite. It asks you to show up as you are, and to trust that’s enough.

There comes a point, at any age, when we get to rediscover our own worth. 
Not the version that performs, or pleases, or fits. 

Just... you.

That’s the practice. To be, rather than to fit in.

From the heart,
Jenny ♥️

Manchmal braucht es nur einen bewussten Moment des Innehaltens, um wieder bei sich selbst anzukommen. ✨

Genau deshalb freue ich mich unglaublich auf das Yoga-Retreat „Bewusst Sein“ in @oberstaufen_allgaeu: vier Tage voller Yoga, Achtsamkeit, Bewegung, Meditation und wertvoller Zeit nur für sich selbst, eingebettet in die wunderschöne Natur des Allgäus. Eine Auszeit, um durchzuatmen, neue Energie zu tanken und Körper und Geist wieder in Einklang zu bringen. ☀️ 🌲 

Ich werde selbst mit dabei sein und kann es kaum erwarten, diese besondere Zeit zu erleben - mit inspirierenden Sessions, Momenten der Ruhe und ganz viel Zeit zum Loslassen, Auftanken und Genießen. 🤍

Das Retreat findet vom 18.–21. Juni statt und umfasst zahlreiche Yoga-, Meditations- und Workshop-Angebote von regionalen Coaches in alpiner Umgebung. ⛰️ Jeder Tag ist einem Element gewidmet.

Das Beste: Aktuell gibt es noch einen Frühbucherrabatt! 😍 Wenn du schon länger spürst, dass dir eine bewusste Pause guttun würde, ist das genau dein Zeichen, dir selbst diese Auszeit zu schenken. Und dann sehen wir uns schon bald auf der Matte in meiner Heimat, dem wunderschönen Allgäu. ☺️

Mehr Infos und den Link zur Buchung findest du in den Stories. Wäre das was für dich? 😊

Couldn’t fit these moments in just 20-30 sec….

But Have you ever wondered who takes care of the people who spend their time caring for others? 

Two beautiful days in Wayanad, Kerala—facilitating a yoga retreat, holding space, sharing practices, and pouring my energy, warmth, focus, and presence into every session.

But in between the schedules and responsibilities, I always remind myself to pause. To breathe. To step outside. To soak in nature, explore places and reconnect with myself.

As yoga teachers, sound healers, and wellness facilitators, we often give so much to others. But we can only pour from a cup that we continue to refill.

I’m deeply grateful for a profession that allows me to meet incredible people, witness meaningful transformations, and travel to places I may never have discovered otherwise.

Feeling thankful, lucky, and humbled by this journey. 

How do you make time for yourself while showing up for everyone else?

Jeetje, wat waren dit weer prachtige dagen.

Voor het derde jaar mocht ik het yoga retreat van Mariska @yogaflowzuidlaren ondersteunen met mijn Reiki-behandelingen.

Vier dagen in de natuur. Vier dagen aandacht voor jezelf, je lichaam en alles wat daarin gevoeld mag worden.

Het blijft bijzonder om te zien hoe mensen aankomen met hun eigen verhaal, verlangens en soms ook wat spanning. En hoe er in een paar dagen tijd meer verbinding met zichzelf en het lichaam ontstaat. 

Het geven van Reiki-behandelingen tijdens zo’n retreat blijft iedere keer weer een mooie ervaring. Het werken met het lichaam is iets waar ik veel passie voor voel.

Ons lichaam is zo wijs. Het vertelt verhalen die soms moeilijk onder woorden te brengen zijn. Tijdens een behandeling laat het vaak precies zien waar aandacht nodig is en waar weer ruimte mag ontstaan. Dat is wat ik mag vertalen.

Het retreat zat weer geweldig in elkaar. Aan elk detail was door Mariska gedacht.

Het kampeerterrein, de prachtige natuurhutten en de toffe caravans maken de beleving van dit retreat extra bijzonder. En het heerlijke eten was iedere keer weer genieten! @voscheheugte

Nog even nagenieten van een prachtig retreat in de natuur!

To set up, situate your elbows approximately at your belly bottom and keep your elbows close. The back is working on overtime - think Salabhasana on steroids :D

Reaching your chest forward is the absolute key - much more than you would expect!  When the legs automatically begin to lengthen and the feet begin to come up by themselves, the chest is approaching an appropriate reach. The toes, too, reach back and the kneecaps are lifted with an engaged quadriceps. In this way the whole body becomes stiff and a pivotting counterbalance sets it.

You will feel that you are close to falling forward, but don’t worry, if you actually fall forward, it is only an inch or two. At the end you can choose to stay horizontal or pivot to a diagonal high-feet position —you choose.

Give it a try (or 100!) - good luck :D

Practicing with good technique means that your Mayurasana will steadily improve. And of course, if you have someone to help you it’s always better! 

This video was filmed with @kinoyoga and @timfeldmannyoga in beautiful Bali.

🕉️ Dol Ashram, Almora — A Spiritual Retreat in the Heart of Kumaon 🏔️✨

Hidden amidst the serene forests of Kumaon, Dol Ashram is a peaceful sanctuary dedicated to yoga, meditation, Vedic wisdom, and spiritual awakening. Founded by Yogi Kalyan Das Ji Maharaj, the ashram is known for its magnificent Shri Yantra Temple, tranquil surroundings, and breathtaking Himalayan views.

📖 History
Established in the 1990s, Dol Ashram was envisioned as a center for spiritual learning and self-discovery. Today, it attracts seekers, meditators, and travelers from across India and abroad who come to experience its unique blend of spirituality and nature.

📍 How to Reach
🚗 Around 40 km from Almora via the Devidhura route.
🚉 Nearest Railway Station: Kathgodam Railway Station (approx. 75–80 km)
✈️ Nearest Airport: Pantnagar Airport (approx. 110 km)
🚕 Taxis are available from Kathgodam, Haldwani, Almora, and Nainital. 📞 Contact @taxi4divine Cabs for bookings and customized Uttarakhand tours.

🌸 Best Time to Visit
☀️ March to June – Pleasant weather and clear mountain views.
🍁 September to November – Lush greenery after monsoon and crisp Himalayan panoramas.
❄️ Winter visits (December–February) offer peaceful vibes and occasional chilly mountain mornings.#dolashram🕉️🔱 #kumaon SpiritualJourney #YogaRetreat #Meditation #uttarakhand

In 1967, a young researcher named Dian Fossey set up two small tents in the Virunga mountains and began watching gorillas no one had ever studied closely before.

She believed mountain gorillas would be extinct by the year 2000.

Today, because of the work she started — carried forward by more than 400 @savinggorillas staff and supporters around the world — mountain gorilla  numbers are actually increasing. It is one of the rare conservation success stories of our time 🙏🏻

This is the organisation whose work we'll be learning about firsthand on our Rwanda and Uganda retreat in May 2027 — visiting their research centre, meeting their team, and then tracking these incredible animals into Bwindi Impenetrable Forest to see first hand, the results of her work which has been pivotal in protecting this iconic species for almost six decades.

If you watched A Gorilla Story on Netflix and you cried a little bit, we did to — this is the retreat for you 🦍 

Details coming to your inbox SOON!!

Il richiamo degli dei, nel Paradiso di Blu, a fine Agosto 🦋

Ci ritroviamo al Mare, presso il nostro rifugio preferito dagli amici del @rifugio_muzzerone_official nel Porto di Venere!

Nel cuore del Golfo dei Poeti : Yoga, Trekking e Meditazione.

Il Rifugio si trova sul Monte Muzzerone, nel lato di fronte all'Isola Palmaria, lungo il sentiero AV5T (Alta Via 5 Terre) e si può raggiungere solo a piedi da Portovenere oppure arrivando da Campiglia, sempre dal sentiero del CAI. Affacciandosi dalla terrazza del Rifugio Muzzerone potrete godere di un’incantevole vista sugli Appennini, su Porto Venere e sull’isola Palmaria, respirando aria fresca e ritemprando il corpo e lo spirito con squisiti primi piatti, secondi e dolci preparati in casa secondo l'antica tradizione ligure.

Ciò che vi offriamo è un weekend di pratiche yoga e trekking in una location mozzafiato, un’occasione per connettersi con sé stessi, con la quiete del luogo e con i propri sensi. Il tutto nel parco naturale di Porto Venere, il quale racchiude nei suoi 400 ettari luoghi di grande valore ambientale, storico e culturale. Il borgo antico, le Isole Palmaria, Tino, Tinetto e l’Area Marina Protetta sono testimonianze preziose di una rapporto armonioso tra uomo e natura. Un sodalizio costruito e consolidato nel tempo che è stato riconosciuto dall’UNESCO con l’istituzione del Sito Porto Venere, Cinque Terre e Isole.

🌱 Day 1: Arrive — Land in the space and unpack the weight of the journey. 

🌱 Day 2: Ground — Roots down. Reconnect to the physical body and the earth. 

🌱 Day 3: Release — Clear out the stagnant density and somatic armor. 

🌱 Day 4: Expand — Create vast space inside the mind, body, and breath. 

🌱 Day 5: Embody — Step fully inside your skin and hold your presence. 

🌱 Day 6: Express — Voice, movement, and unedited truth. 

🌱 Day 7: Integrate — Settle the experiences and stitch them into your timeline. 

🌱 Day 8: Emerge — Step out of the container renewed, ready to head home.

Every layer is held by master facilitators who specialize in safety and containment.

NIDRA YOGA 🌙 LUCID DREAMING WORKSHOP
✨2 Tage + 1 Nacht im Traumtempel✨22-23 Aug in Gaildorf✨

Verschläfst du EIN DRITTEL DEINES LEBENS oder wirst du schon wach?
In diesem Workshop erforschen wir NIDRA YOGA und das TIBETISCHE YOGA DES KLAREN LICHTS, kraftvolle Wege der Bewusstseinserweiterung.
Nidra Yoga wird heute oft mit angeleiteter Entspannung gleichgesetzt, doch dies ist nur ein sehr oberflächlicher Teil dessen, was wir im Event praktizieren. Wir erforschen den „schlaflosen“ YOGISCHEN SCHLAF, traditionelle + moderne Techniken des LUZIDEN TRÄUMENS sowie INTENTIONSARBEIT im Astralraum. Wir zielen dabei auf Bewusstsein in allen Phasen des Schlafes. Es ist ein intensives Wochenende, das dennoch tief entspannend wirken kann und uns hilft uns selbst und dem Selbst tiefer zu begegnen.
Der Kurs ist dabei kein hipper Ausflug in rosarote Traumwelten. Wir möchten nicht der Realität entkommen, sondern ihr mit yogischer Praxis klar begegnen, und dies im Sinne der Nidra Yoga Tradition bzw. des Tibetischen Yoga des Klaren Lichts.

🌺 DU BEKOMMST
▶ Hatha-Kundalini Yoga-Sessions, konzipiert für tiefe Wahrnehmung, mehr Bewusstsein & Öffnung zur Traumwelt (Anfänger & Fortgeschrittene) mit Fokus auf Achtsamkeit & Energiefluss
▶ Praktische Übungen & Meditationen zum Vertiefen der theoretischen Inhalte
▶ Einführungen in Nidra Yoga, Arbeit mit Intention im Astralraum
▶ Angeleitete Yoga Nidra Sessions
▶ Zeit für Fragen & Austausch, Integrationsübungen für den Tag
▶ Optionale Nacht im Traumtempel: Gemeinsame Übernachtung im Yogaraum & nachts mit Gruppenenergie praktizieren 

🌺 ANMELDUNG
Sei WILLKOMMEN wie du bist - als Paar, Single, jung & alt.
Neugier und Offenheit in Herz & Geist sind hilfreich, ansonsten benötigst du keine Vorkenntnisse. 
Der Workshop findet auf SPENDENBASIS statt weil wir überzeugt sind, dass niemand aus finanziellem Grund von diesem wertvollen Wissen ausgeschlossen werden soll.
Zur TEILNAHME benötigst du deine klare Absicht, eine Anmeldung per Mail, und eine Anzahlung von 85€ für die Verbindlichkeit. Mehr INFO im Profillink.

Im November werden die Tage langsam wieder kürzer und die Welt draußen verändert sich. 
 
Mit diesem besonderen Retreat im Herbst laden wir dich und deine Freundin ein, an diesem wunderschönen Ort inmitten der Natur euer Herz zu öffnen, zur Ruhe zu kommen und eure Freundschaft auf eine noch tiefere Weise zu erleben.
​
​Zwischen sanften Yoga-Flows, aktivierenden Einheiten, Momenten der Reflexion sowie Meditation und Entspannung entsteht ein Raum, in dem ihr loslassen und ganz bei euch ankommen dürft.
​
Ein besonderes Highlight ist unsere Kakaozeremonie am Freitagabend. Sie lädt euch ein, nach dem Ankommen innezuhalten und euer Herz zu öffnen.

Am Samstag erwartet euch eine wohltuende Mischung aus Energie und Entschleunigung: ein kraftvoller Morgenflow, Journaling, eine achtsame Gehmeditation in der bezaubernden Natur und tiefe Entspannung.
​
Dieses Wochenende gehört euch. Nehmt die Einladung an und lasst uns gemeinsam lachen, ankommen und bewusster werden.

🌿 Gönn dir eine Auszeit, die mehr ist als Urlaub. 🌿

Spürst du, wie die Anforderungen des Alltags immer mehr Energie kosten? Dann schenke dir eine Woche voller Ruhe, Natur und neuer Kraft an der sonnigen Costa del Sol.

In Málaga erwarten dich achtsame Naturerlebnisse, wohltuendes Waldbaden, tägliche Hatha-Yoga-Einheiten und wertvolle Impulse für deine mentale und körperliche Gesundheit. Entdecke den Biophilia-Effekt – die nachweislich positive Wirkung von Natur auf Stressabbau, Regeneration und Wohlbefinden.
Zwischen grünen Oasen, botanischen Gärten und den malerischen weißen Dörfern Andalusiens findest du zurück zu mehr Gelassenheit, innerer Balance und Lebensfreude. Die erlernten Methoden begleiten dich weit über die Reise hinaus – in deinen Alltag, deinen Garten, den Wald oder dein Zuhause.

✨ Deine Zeit zum Durchatmen.
✨ Deine Zeit zum Auftanken.
✨ Deine Zeit für dich.

Besonders attraktiv: Im Rahmen des Bildungsurlaubs findet ein zertifizierter Präventionskurs statt, der von vielen Krankenkassen bezuschusst wird.

🌱 Entdecke die Kraft der Natur und stärke Körper, Geist und Seele nachhaltig.

Trikonasana (Triangle Pose) is much more than a physical stretch. It creates a feeling of expansion, grounding, and openness throughout the body and mind.

The triangle is considered one of the most stable geometric shapes. In Trikonasana, we embody:

Grounding (legs)
Openness (heart)
Vision (gaze)

It can be a beautiful pose to practice when you’re navigating life transitions, seeking clarity, or reconnecting with your own inner strength and direction.

“Where in my life am I being asked to stand strong while remaining open?”
Ask yourself this question and journal about it. See what comes up.

I am happiest dripping wet, sun-soaked, surf-stoked. Having now experienced the retreat first-hand, I truly can't wait for @GaySurfWeeks this May and September. Between my poetry book and these retreats, I'm anxious to appear salesy, but please know my heart is firmly rooted in community and inviting deserving hearts into it. Here are some highlights from my week at @surfsynergy: 

•I caught more waves in five days than in 5 years and that's no exaggeration. The 1-on-1 surf coaching really is that girl.
•Everyone at Surf Synergy! Truly the best surf staff I've ever come across! Their expressions of excitement for Gay Surf Week already made it feel like home. 
•The food! Sometimes meals at all-inclusive offerings can be a bit bland, but it was so delicious and far surpassed anything I ate outside the retreat. 
•I had never dedicated a whole week to wellness before. Yoga, ice baths, breathwork, massage, and surfing felt like some sort of cheat code.
•Watching newcomers fall in love with the sport of surfing. Seeing new friends transforming their minds and bodies atop water is an all-time honor!

I'm busy scheming about all the extra queer fun that'll we'll add to the retreats as we take them over completely! Just a little over a month until the first one in May (few spots left) and bookings are now open for September!

✨ Healing isn’t linear… and that’s okay.

So often we think healing means:
therapy ➡️ yoga ➡️ breathwork ➡️ plant medicine ➡️ and boom… we’re healed.

But in reality, it’s more like:
you go on retreat, practice yoga, feel good… then a message from your mom sends you spiraling back to feeling 5 years old and unloved. You try a new modality and feel a breakthrough… then an old trauma from your teens resurfaces.

Healing is not all rainbows and roses 🌈🌹—it’s messy, layered, and full of ups and downs.

What I’ve learned on my own journey is that healing is the process – the ups and the downs. And even in the tough moments, you can still find love, joy, and connection with the people who truly care about you.

This is why I’m so passionate about holding space for others through breathwork and retreats – because you don’t have to navigate it all alone. We heal together. 🤍

Thank you @josh_ffw for speaking this truth so beautifully. 🙏

#healingjourney #breathwork #selfhealing #spiritualjourney #traumahealing #innerwork #healingisnotlinear #emotionalhealing #awakening #retreatlife #somabreath #nervoussystemregulation #healingtogether #mindbodyspirit

🧠 - Neuroplasticity & Life long learning: Take a look at these 4 multipolar neurons. Each has been influenced by exposure to different learning experiences, different expressions of movement, different emotions & / or different sensory stimuli. These experiences have caused each of these similarly typed neurons to form their myriad of connections in vastly different ways & as such, they transmit information differently to the neural circuits within which each one is a part of.  @the.brain.guy

__

🧠 - When we enrich our lives with new information, be it: sensory, emotional, movement oriented, or intellectual, we create new connections between neurons in the brain & we effectively enhance our brain cells. Research shows that life-long learning will effectively increase your brain power. What this means is that learning anything new enhances your memory, increases the robustness of neural circuits & can even form new brain cells too!
__

🧠 - On the flip side of that coin however, when you stop learning & simply commit to a life of habitual experience, areas of your brain literally begin to shrink! Once again, it comes down to energy & survival: The brain is incredibly intelligent in the way it goes about maximising it’s (your!) survival chances. When it detects that certain neural connections are not frequently in use, it quite literally prunes those connections so as to not waste energy maintaining connections that you’re not actively using & reinforcing. Committing to life long learning is an incredible thing that you can do for your own mental, physical & emotional health. Use it or lose it!

Want to form millions of new connections that lead you to an enhanced future version of yourself? Novel experiences, information, movement, thought & environment are key!

📣📣ANNOUNCING the official brand reveal of “El Refugio at The Trapp Family Farm!! 📣📣

As many of you have been following this new adventure we are on, you’ve seen that I’ve been calling in my “mountain-magic” property for a while now. But when this gem landed on our lap quite suddenly, we followed the undeniable flips in our bellies and went all in! July 23rd was the day that we became the stewards of this land and we were most excited about the surprises that we knew were waiting for us! 
—
Upon arrival, we were gifted a book on the history of the area and were ecstatic to learn that our land (particularly the pond and the barn), has been referred to as “el Refugio” (“The refuge”), since the early 1940’s.
—
When I think about what was happening in the world at that time, things feel familiar. The fact that this was a place for animals to safely seek water, food, and shelter, and also a place for wanderers to tie up their horses and rest for the night….everything feels perfect and not a coincidence at all. 

We have a beautiful vision that will welcome our first guests to “El Refugio” early next summer. Flowers 🌺, organically grown food, and an opportunity to sleep alongside the rhythms of mama earth, are all on the schedule!
__
Retreats will be posted *very soon but I wanted to give you a sneak peek of my Pinterest board here!! (But of course, knowing me there will be MANY surprises 😉😆).
And we always have our ears to the ground for any amazing ideas that you may want to pin to our board too! Feel free to share them below! ⬇️

Sending you all love!
M

This week has been a beautiful reminder that there is always something to learn.

I’ve been back at the stunning Cortijo San José, offering Spinal Flow Technique and massage for a wonderful group led by Amy McDonald and organised by YOAS Yoga Retreats.

While I absolutely love holding my own retreats, there is something equally valuable about stepping into someone else’s retreat space. Watching different facilitators, experiencing different energies and learning from different approaches keeps me inspired, curious and growing.

For me, retreats are never *just* about yoga, healing sessions or beautiful locations. They’re about the people, the connections and the unique way each leader creates an experience that feels entirely their own.

And whilst I’m not sharing all the details just yet, spending time alongside another YOAS retreat has felt especially exciting because next year I’ll be working not only as a healing therapist but also as one of the retreat leaders for YOAS.

More on that soon... ✨

For now, I’m full to the brim with the conversations, the healing work and the privilege of being part of another wonderful week in this special place.

For most of our lives, we’re known by our roles.
A wife. A mother. An employee. A business owner. A carer. A helper. And somewhere between school runs, deadlines, family responsibilities and endless to-do lists, many women forget that before all those roles, they were simply women. #WomensWellness

Women with dreams that had nothing to do with paying bills or packing lunches.
The older I get, the more I realise how important it is to keep discovering new parts of ourselves.
At 49, I started exploring modelling and background acting. Not because I needed a new career. Not because I wanted to prove anything.
But because I wanted to remind myself that growth doesn’t have an expiry date. Age is not a deadline.
It’s permission. Permission to try. Permission to learn. Permission to reinvent yourself. Permission to become more of who you already are.
This is what Treats & Retreats is really about.
Not yoga.
Not wellness trends.
Not becoming a different person.
It’s about creating space for women to reconnect with themselves and remember that they are allowed to have dreams, adventures, passions and desires at every stage of life.
You are more than your responsibilities.
And it’s never too late to discover what else is possible.

✨ Meet Your Hosts ✨

Nicola, co-founder of Olea Voyages - a luxury boutique travel agency, was born in the UK and moved to France as a teenager. She has since made it her home for over 25 years, and today she shares its beauty and lifestyle with travellers from around the world.

A passionate yoga practitioner and wellness enthusiast, she is often on the mat alongside Céline, her teacher and friend. This retreat brings together her love of yoga, travel, and creating meaningful experiences, as well as her passion for helping others discover the beauty, rhythm, and slower way of life of the place she now calls home.

Céline discovered yoga later in life, after a childhood spent practising gymnastics. Drawn to the depth of the practice beyond the physical postures, she completed her 200-hour teacher training to deepen her understanding of yoga philosophy, alignment, and tradition.

Today, she teaches from her own studio, offering Vinyasa, Ashtanga, and Yin Yoga, with classes shaped by presence, precision, and an intuitive understanding of her students’ needs and energy.

Both Nicola and Céline are bon vivants at heart, with a shared love of good food, French wine, long tables, music, and dancing. Olea Retreat is the expression of this shared joy: movement, connection, and the art of slowing down in beautiful places.

We can’t wait to welcome you!

The reality of building Goat Rodeo Retreats isn’t just paint colors, renovations, and pretty reveal photos.

There were plenty of moments where the overwhelm felt bigger than the vision. Looking at everything that needed to be done before opening could easily send me into a spiral of timelines, to-do lists, and the feeling that we needed to work faster.

But here’s what surprised me most: if I pushed harder, stressed more, and rushed the process, I’d be bulldozing the very reason we’re creating this place in the first place.

Goat Rodeo Retreats is meant to be a space for slowing down, reconnecting, and finding rest. Building it has required me to practice those same things myself.

In many ways, creating this retreat has been a mirror, showing me where I need to slow my own nervous system, trust the process, and take the work one day at a time.

And for anyone wondering what was unexpectedly difficult...

Choosing bed linens. 😅

Seriously. We spent an embarrassing amount of time thinking about sheets, blankets, textures, and comfort. So when you sink into bed after a day at the retreat, just know your comfort has been considered in every possible way.

The details matter because you matter. 🤍

One of my favourite things about retreat isn’t what happens in Bali.

It’s what happens when people get home.

The messages that say:

“I’ve started walking every morning again.”

“I finally had the conversation I’d been avoiding.”

“I’m setting boundaries without feeling guilty.”

“I realised I don’t have to keep living the way I was living.”

My goal was never to create just an amazing week away.

The goal has always been to create change that follows you home…

My loves, real change isn’t what happens during a breathwork session, or a meditation, or a sound healing.

It’s what happens when you return to your everyday life and start making different choices because you see yourself differently.

That’s the part I love most! 

Watching someone arrive exhausted, overwhelmed and disconnected… then leave remembering who they are… gets me every time 🥹

I have been so present on this retreat that I barely picked up my phone, which feels like exactly the point!

Day one in Adelboden started early with a sunrise reset before breakfast looking out over the Alps. From there we took the cable car up to Engstligenalp for a waterfall hike that none of us were prepared for in the best way. Mountains like that have a way of going quiet in your mind.

The afternoon moved between e-bikes and the water with swim techniques and diving basics taught by genuine Olympic level coaches, the kind of access you do not get anywhere else!

We ended the day on the Spa Terrace as the light dropped over the peaks and then sat down to a four course dinner with a Q&A that turned into one of those conversations you remember for a long time.

I am home now and still processing how much happened in just one day, so I am going to share more from this trip over the next few days because there is too much to put into one post.

More to come 🦔

Step into the quiet tranquility and vibrant energy of Song of the Morning as you explore the shrine trails that wind throughout our 800-acre forest sanctuary.

These peaceful paths lead through forests, wetlands, meadows, and along the beautiful Pigeon River, inviting you to slow down, breathe deeply, and reconnect with the beauty and stillness of the natural world. Each trail leads to a sacred space where you can pause, reflect, meditate, pray, journal, or simply immerse yourself in Mother Nature’s gentle embrace.

Enjoy forest vistas and river views, towering pines and moss-covered pathways, scenic overlooks, and secluded shrines. Along the way, you may be greeted by white-tailed deer, sandhill cranes, bald eagles, butterflies, and other wildlife who make their home here.

Whether you spend a few quiet moments on a bench overlooking the river or an afternoon exploring the trails, each path offers an opportunity to experience the sacred presence that surrounds us and resides within us.

*"Whenever you feel the leaves moving and swaying, feel the gentle whisper of God. It is God’s breath that has touched the leaves which whisper to us of the gentleness of His presence."*

— Paramahansa Yogananda

This weekend, we welcome another beautiful group of guests to Italy for our June Cleanse Retreat.

As we prepare welcome packs, supplements, finalise the details, and get ready to hold space for the week ahead, we’re reflecting on what a privilege this work truly is.

Every retreat begins the same way: a group of people arriving from different walks of life.

Some arrive exhausted.
Some overwhelmed.
Some searching for answers.
Some simply knowing that something needs to change.

And then something beautiful unfolds.

New friendships are formed.
Energy begins to return.
Perspectives shift.
People reconnect with themselves in ways they never expected.

Because the transformation isn’t really about the cold pressed juice, the treatments, the workshops, or even the breathtaking Tuscan surroundings.

It’s about creating the space to pause.To listen. To reconnect. To remember who you are beneath the noise of everyday life.

As we welcome our June guests, we’re reminded that transformation often begins with one simple decision:

The decision to choose yourself.

So wherever you are today, we invite you to reflect:

• Where in my life am I being called to create more space?
• What part of myself am I ready to reconnect with?
• What would it look like to truly prioritise my wellbeing?

To our June retreat guests, we cannot wait to welcome you.

And to our wider community, thank you for walking this journey with us.

With love from Tuscany 🇮🇹✨

Tina & Jess xx

Hey, my name is Coco. 

For those who remember Cuba Yoga, this is the story of what’s changed and what hasn’t.

For nearly 10 years I ran the first international retreat center in Havana. We hosted almost 200 retreats, built community, and shared in Cuban culture. It shaped me and it shaped this page.

Then life shifted. I had to start over. 

This reel explains that transition and why this account is now Alba Retreats.

Today I work with retreat leaders and properties around the world. I help them design, price, and fill retreats that succeed because they are built on trust, local culture, and real connection.

If you’ve been part of this journey since Cuba, thank you 🤍❤️💙. 

And if you’re new, welcome. What I’ll share here is rooted in the same values that carried me from Havana until now: connection, community, humanity, and the strategies that make retreats thrive.

Follow along if you are ready to create retreats that work.

✨ Preparing for the ritual is the ritual. ✨

Long before the first welcome circle begins, before the first yoga practice is shared, before the first deep exhale of retreat… the work has already begun.

Every mat placed. Every prop arranged. Every candle lit. Every detail considered with intention.

At GetAway Yogis, we believe that creating a transformative retreat experience starts long before you arrive. From the moment you register to the moment you return home, our goal is for you to feel cared for, supported, and held.

We spend months—and often years—planning these experiences because we know that when people feel safe, they can soften. When they feel supported, they can let go. When they feel nurtured, they can reconnect with themselves in a deeper way.

Whether it’s accommodating dietary needs, creating beautiful practice spaces, curating meaningful cultural experiences, or simply remembering how you take your coffee, we do our very best to ensure that every guest feels seen.

Because retreat isn’t just about where we go. It’s about how we make people feel while they’re there.

Last weekend, I did something I’ve always wanted to do that I really never thought I could and I’m amazed at the abundance of the universe 🙏🏼

I hosted my very first retreat in Arizona and my highest intention was to create a conscious space that invited women from all over the country to do the inner work, release old layers, and come home to themselves. 

It was pure magic 🪄 

We moved, we breathed, we nourished, we questioned, we learned, we re-wired, we played, we released, we activated….

…we healed ❤️

And the strongest healing, in my experience, happens in community. Within dialogue. 

Just being around other women, energy alone, shifts the timeline.

Toss in the homemade with love vegan meals, Arizona sunshine, high vibe conversations, and intentional space and time for rest and connection…it’s a recipe for a quantum leap 💫 

It’s when we are stuck in reverse, repeating patterns and looping over and over again in our minds that we begin to feed the darkness. 

We become disconnected from our light. Our truth. 

To come back to wholeness, we need love. Lots and lots of love. Rest, too. 

And the courage to look at what’s going on in your 3D life and ask, “What if this was for me?” 

I am still on such a high and essentially love drunk from last weekend and it only confirmed that I am exactly where I’m supposed to be ✨

Who’s coming to the next one?! 🙋🏻‍♀️

In a world shaken by conflict, famine, and disconnection, now more than ever, we are called to turn inward — to return to the heart. Practices like meditation, remembrance (dhikr), and spiritual awareness are not luxuries — they are necessities. They anchor us in peace, unite us in love, and awaken our responsibility to serve.

Join us on a sacred retreat through some of the most spiritually charged places on Earth: Istanbul, Konya, and Cappadocia, where centuries of devotion still echo in every stone and breeze.

Together, we will:
✨ Meditate beneath the domes of Aya Sophia and the Blue Mosque
✨ Offer prayers for peace at the tomb of Mevlana Rumi, whose timeless words call us to love
✨ Sit in silent remembrance at the tomb of Shams of Tabriz, igniting the fire of divine union
✨ Visit the sanctuary of Haji Bektash Veli, honoring the path of wisdom and service

This is not only a retreat — it is an act of service.

I just reactivated ‘Time Hop’ after being off social media for a while and all my old yoga videos are beginning to trickle through. 

This is from 2 years ago … not long before I got pregnant.

I used to love a strong practice and was a self confessed arm balance junkie but these days I’m more concerned with teddy bears than crow pose! 

Motherhood changes us … I believe for the better. One year post partum and ‘pregnant me’ was convinced I’d ‘have my practice back’ (whatever that means!). But … I’ve spent the past 12 months feeding a human from my body, carrying her around (hello back pain!), and waking up every time my beautiful baby’s girl so much as stirs 💕

The time spent off my mat and with my daughter I will cherish for an eternity. Nailing a press handstand pails in comparison to watching her take her first steps and say her first words.

For her first birthday a good friend brought her her first toddler yoga mat 🥹 and have a little 18 month yoga set waiting patiently in the closet. I am so excited to introduce her to yoga when she shows interest!

This Sunday we fly out to Goa to greet 25 gorgeous adults and (3 babies!) for my long awaited India retreat. What a beautiful way to kick of our 2023 program!

Today I’m feeling inspired to unroll my mat for the first time in a while. I’ll wait until India is up from her nap as I’m sure it will be so entertaining for her … and … we’ll just see what unfolds. No pressure, no expectations, just pure presence 💕

Per anni ho pensato che, scegliendo il mondo olistico, stessi lasciando alle spalle una parte importante di me.

La programmatrice.

Il codice.

La logica.

La capacità di creare strutture.

Pensavo che appartenessero a una vita precedente.

Poi ho capito una cosa.

I talenti non si perdono. Si trasformano.

Oggi non scrivo più programmi per le aziende.

Ma quella parte di me continua a vivere.

È proprio grazie alla mia mente da programmatrice che ho potuto strutturare la Mappa dei Talenti: uno strumento visivo che, partendo dalla data di nascita, aiuta a riconoscere talenti, risorse e potenzialità che spesso abbiamo sempre avuto… senza rendercene conto.

Nel mio lavoro, però, non utilizzo un solo strumento.

La Mappa dei Talenti e i Tarocchi dialogano tra loro.

La mappa offre una visione d’insieme, mettendo in luce i talenti e le qualità della persona.

I tarocchi aiutano ad ascoltare ciò che in quel momento chiede attenzione, portando consapevolezza e nuove prospettive.

È proprio dall’incontro tra questi strumenti, unito al lavoro esperienziale che vivremo insieme, che nasce “Dai Talenti al Valore”.

Non è un ritiro per dirti cosa fare della tua vita.

È un’esperienza per fermarti, riconoscere ciò che hai dentro e iniziare a trasformare i tuoi talenti in valore, per te e per gli altri.

Perché, a volte, il talento che stai cercando… è quello che ti accompagna da sempre, ma aspetta soltanto di essere visto. ✨

✨ Feeling incredibly grateful. ✨

This weekend, I had the privilege of facilitating and organizing our retreat for the third time. Every retreat is special, but taking a moment to reflect on how much we’ve built together over the past 1.5 years fills me with deep gratitude.

From creating all the content, videos, and photography, to guiding breathwork sessions, ice baths, and holding space for the conversations that unfold throughout the weekend. It’s an honor to be part of these transformative experiences.

What touches me most is witnessing people arrive in one state and leave in a completely different one. More connected to themselves, more present, more alive, and often with a renewed perspective on what truly matters.

Seeing the growth, trust, and community that have emerged around these retreats has been beyond anything I could have imagined. None of this would be possible without every person who has chosen to show up, open up, and be part of this journey. And a special S/O to @renshaen & @mink_de_jong 🫶

And it feels like we’re only getting started. There is still so much beauty, growth, and transformation ahead.

Thank you to everyone who has been part of this story so far. 🤍

Goat Rodeo Retreats started long before the renovations, the animals, or the retreats.

It started with burnout.

After years of working in the corporate world, constantly moving from one deadline to the next, I realized I was craving something different. More time outside. More meaningful connection. More space to slow down and actually enjoy life instead of rushing through it.

When we found this property, we didn’t just see land and buildings—we saw possibility. A place where people could step away from the noise, catch their breath, gather with people they love, and reconnect with themselves.

Building Goat Rodeo Retreats meant leaving behind what no longer felt aligned and creating something that did.

Today, every retreat, every gathering, and every guest who walks through our doors is a reminder of why we took the leap.

This place became the answer we were looking for, and now we get to share it with others. 🤍

Being on a retreat for retreat leaders with @iamfit4travel is truly one of the greatest gifts.

Five days surrounded by people who do what I do—sharing ideas, exchanging experiences, finding inspiration, resting, recharging, and building meaningful connections in the stunning Algarve. I honestly have no words to express how grateful I am.

Sometimes I still can't believe this is my life.

And if anything, this experience has inspired me even more to continue doing what I do. Being on the receiving end of a retreat reminds me just how important it is to step away from daily responsibilities, disconnect from the noise, recharge, and open ourselves to new friendships and perspectives.

What a gift.

Thank you, Fit4Travel, for making this experience possible. My heart is full. 💙✨

🌊✨ Introducing *Water Reiki* ✨🌊

Since 2011, I have been devoted to developing *Water Reiki* in the sacred waters of the Narmada River — a deeply transformative practice designed to harmonize the body, calm the mind, and awaken the spirit.

Floating in water allows us to release emotional weight, dissolve stress, and reconnect with the natural flow within ourselves. Through gentle movement, meditation, breath, and energy healing, Water Reiki creates a space of deep surrender, healing, and inner peace. 💧🕊️

I am now looking to collaborate with wellness spaces, retreat centers, yoga communities, healers, and conscious facilitators around the world to share this unique experience globally.

📍 Ideal space: Swimming pool with approx. 1.5 meter depth
🤍 Suitable for retreats, healing workshops, yoga festivals & wellness events

If you feel called to co-create or host a Water Reiki experience, I would love to connect.

Let the water hold you.
Let the flow heal you.
Let go, and return to yourself. 🌊✨

✨ The Difference Between Visiting Cambodia and Experiencing It ✨

There is a difference between arriving somewhere and truly meeting it.

Most travel is about seeing a place. Visiting landmarks. Taking photos. Collecting memories.

But there is another way to travel.

One that is less about movement and more about presence. 🌿

When we rush from one experience to the next, we often remain unchanged. We see a place, but we do not fully feel it. We pass through it, but it never passes through us.

Experiencing a place is different.

It happens when you slow down enough to listen.

When the temples become more than attractions 🛕✨

When silence becomes part of the journey.

When the landscape begins reflecting something back to you.

The difference between visiting and experiencing is not the destination.

It is the intention you bring.

At The Temple Within Retreat, Cambodia is not something to tick off a list.

It is an invitation to slow down, reconnect, and allow the outer journey to become an inner one. 🤍

Because sometimes the most meaningful discoveries are not found in the places we visit, but in the parts of ourselves we meet while we are there.

To visit a place is to leave with memories.

To experience a place is to leave with change.

✨ The Temple Within Retreat | Cambodia | June 2027 ✨

✨ 5 Ways to Support Your Integration After a Spiritual Retreat ✨

A spiritual retreat doesn’t end when you leave the sacred space. The real transformation often happens during the integration process.

🌿 1. Spend Time in Nature
Ground your energy by walking barefoot on the earth, sitting under a tree, or spending quiet time outdoors.

📖 2. Journal Your Experiences
Write down insights, dreams, messages, emotions, and synchronicities before they fade. Your journal becomes a bridge between the retreat and daily life.

💧 3. Hydrate & Nourish Your Body
Spiritual work can be energetically intense. Drink plenty of water and choose foods that help your body feel supported and balanced.

🧘🏽‍♀️ 4. Create Quiet Time for Reflection
Avoid jumping immediately back into a busy schedule. Meditation, breathwork, prayer, or simply sitting in silence can help you process what was received.

💜 5. Work with Your Retreat Tools
Continue using any crystals, affirmations, oracle cards, worksheets, or practices that resonated with you during the retreat. Consistency helps anchor the energy.

✨ Remember: Integration is where wisdom becomes transformation. Be patient with yourself as your mind, body, and spirit align with your new awareness.

On Tuesday I welcomed a 1:1 private retreat guest to a BRAND NEW venue.

And honestly it has been hiding in plain sight for months. 😄🌿

You might remember last year I spent a few days at Rob's cabin in silence to write my book. It was extraordinary. Quiet. Peaceful. Completely removed from the world.

And somehow it only just dawned on me that this was the perfect space for a private retreat. 🏡 The Asha Centre is almost fully booked with retreats right now.

And I had a very special guest who really deserved a solo break and some proper TLC.

Once the idea landed, everything fell into place beautifully. 💫 She is staying at the cabin for three nights. And I am there morning and afternoon each day to provide a treatment and some exploration into mindfulness and meditation for stress management.

Alongside our time together I have also provided:

🌿 Journaling instructions and prompts
🧘 Self-led mindfulness exercises
🤸 Simple stretching sequences she can use in her own time

The cabin itself is stunning. Beautiful grounds and garden. A little spring and stream running through the land. And some scowles too, those extraordinary geological rock features unique to the Forest of Dean. 🌳

Retreat guests can opt for total silence. Or a full digital detox. Or something in between. The key is that we create something perfect for each individual person. So they get exactly what they need. Nothing more. Nothing less.

I cannot wait to see her later today and continue the unravelling journey together. If a personal 1:1 retreat has been on your mind… This might be your sign.

🌅 IN-Tention | Radiant Rise Retreat
Day 1: RECEIVE & RETURN

The retreat began long before the workshops, breathwork, or healing sessions.

It began with a single word.

✨ RECEIVE ✨

"You are worthy of what you so freely give."

As each beautiful woman arrived, she was welcomed into a space intentionally created for her to slow down, soften, and receive.

Each guest received a curated gift bag filled with meaningful treasures:
📖 A personalized journal with a handwritten message and special word chosen just for her.
💎 A necklace featuring stones selected to reflect her unique journey, including Howlite, Blue Tiger Eye, Lapis Lazuli, Citrine, Pink Opal, and Rose Quartz.
✨ A piece of Selenite, our collective stone of light, clarity, and renewal.
☕ An IN-Tention keepsake cup inscribed with the words:
"Return to Yourself."

"You don't have to hold it all here."

After sharing a nourishing meal and sweet pineapple dessert, we walked to the dock together and watched the sunset over the water, taking in the beauty of the moment and preparing our hearts for what was yet to come.

This was only the beginning.

🌅 Return to yourself.
🌿 Reconnect to your why.
🦋 Rise in your light.

The Yoga Forest found me by chance in 2019. My lifestyle in those years had been quite crazy, I would commonly overwork myself and shoot 14+ hours a day, many days in a row. Some weeks I even devoted over 100 hours to work! It never felt like a task because I love what I do, but the physical exhaustion still took a toll on my body. Eventually, I herniated two vertebras in my lower back and was faced with a harsh reality: my back was broken at 24 years old.
 
Doctors recommended surgery, drugs and injections. I knew I needed something else. I needed a change in lifestyle and habits, and most importantly, I needed to learn to listen to my body.

I looked up yoga retreats on Google while I was at the gate waiting on a flight back to Montreal from Calgary, after a week of production for a corporate videos series. I had three free weeks ahead and wanted to leave the next day. I was looking anywhere in the world: India, Bali, Argentina… I chose Guatemala right the instant I saw a picture of the main yoga platform overlooking the volcanos of Lake Atitlan. Mind you, at this time I had never practiced yoga, not even once.

I booked flights and a one-week retreat and wrote an email to the owners asking if they’d host and teach me in exchange for video production. I showed up in San Jose la Laguna in Lake Atitlan, Guatemala 36 hours later, before they could even reply to my email, and I had a most revealing experience around the lake.

To this day, yoga is part of my life and I credit it for much of my current health, fitness and mental clarity.
 
Enjoy this little souvenir video. Music recorded on location during a song ceremony (kirtan)

İnsan ancak kalpten gelen bir niyetin yolunda yorulmaz. Ve hep hatırla; süreçteki en zor şey ilk adımı atmaktır. Everything starts with an intention. Many years ago, we has an intention; we looked for a place, we worked on the project of @kiooretreatcenter for years. Finally bought land!  I'm thrilled to announce that I've just become the proud owner of ocean-view 3-acre property in Puerto Rico! 🌴 Together with my dear friend, who owns 4 acres next door, we're embarking on an incredible journey to create a farm and retreat center that will inspire and nurture both the land and the soul.  This paradise is already blessed with a diverse range of fruit trees, including luscious mango, exotic mamey sapote, delightful banana, creamy avocado, refreshing coconut, noni and breadfruit.  The abundance of these natural treasures and many more that will be planted will undoubtedly bring endless joy and nourishment to all who visit our farm.

One of the most enchanting features of our property is the year-round stream that gracefully flows at the bottom.  This precious water source will not only sustain crops and support irrigation but also provide a soothing backdrop for relaxation and reflection. There's something truly magical about the symphony of flowing water harmonizing with the rustling leaves and chirping birds.

The property's gentle slope presents an incredible opportunity for farming.  With this natural advantage, we can employ innovative techniques like terracing and contour farming to maximize efficiency and yield. By harnessing the power of gravity, we'll ensure proper water drainage and nutrient distribution, fostering a thriving ecosystem for our crops to flourish.

Our dream is to create a haven where people can reconnect with nature, learn sustainable growing practices, and find solace in the tranquility of this picturesque landscape. We envision hosting workshops and retreats that promote holistic well-being and ecological stewardship.

ENG in comments ⬇️ Когда я приехала в медитационный центр при Ват Пхра Тхат Дой Сутхеп рядом с Чиангмаем, я не имела никакого представления, что такое медитировать. Я увидела на сайте центра "Установочный курс медитации Випассана, 21день" и послала заявку. 

Существует мнение, что медитация – это успокоение и релаксация, но это не мой случай. Первый опыт випассаны был подобен прорыву плотины на мощной сибирской реке: меня чуть не унесло ревущей энергией и не разорвало всем тем, что годы копилось у меня внутри. 
Курсы випассаны, как она практикуется в Северном Таиланде, построены таким образом, что за короткий промежуток времени ты переживаешь максимально возможную гамму состояний, как физических, так эмоциональных, для того чтобы  очиститься и выйти на финальную стадию в нейтральном состоянии ума.

Когда выходят санкхары, бессознательные отпечатки наших предыдущих действий и опытов, это часто сопряжено с сильной физической и эмоциональной болью.

Было трудно, но когда я закончила ретрит и покинула центр, мне казалось, что я родилась заново. Ту дату, 31 марта, я стала считать своим вторым днем рождения, а Чиангмай – второй родиной. Мне в этом году исполнилось пятнадцать. Хороший возраст для чего угодно.

На фотографиях:
1. Комната, где мы ежедневно отчитывались перед учителем. 
2. Здание старого медитационного центра, где я медитировала первый раз. Новый центр более поместительный. 
3. Я медитирую в пустом зале для медитации. 
4. Открытые окна в зале.
5. Джунгли за этими окнами. 
6. Вид на Чиангмай из монастыря.
7. Модель с обозначением точек, на которых надо сосредотачиваться. 

Если кому-то хочется узнать больше, дайте знать. Я напишу еще пост или поделюсь опытом в личных сообщениях.

A new series with Booboo and Marta. As Marta grows older, Booboo tries to teach her the fundamentals and simple realities of life. 

The first lesson is this threefold gradual training. This training is the whole of the spiritual path. All teachings are contained in sila, samadhi and panna. It is a lifelong training and the final goal of this training is true happiness.

The three are like a tripod, all are essentials in the cultivation, with morality as the main foundation.

Often people seek higher wisdom, insight and deep meditation etc, totally omitting the training of morality. But without a strong foundation, one cannot go too far in spiritual path.

Thus by gradually cultivating sila, samadhi, panna, this spiritual path will eventually lead to true happiness.

In this tradition, dreaming is not separate from the dieta.
It is part of the relationship.

Not all healing happens in five days.

But five days can be enough to uncover patterns you’ve been carrying for years.
To see what has been hidden beneath the surface.
To understand where certain struggles, emotions, or dynamics may truly come from.

And sometimes, that awareness alone creates the first movement towards lasting change.

This retreat isn’t about finding a quick fix.

It’s about creating the conditions for real transformation.

Through Family Constellation work, supported by yoga, meditation, breathwork, TRE and other integrative practices, we create a space where healing can unfold on a deeper level. A space to explore what is ready to be seen, felt, released, and integrated. 

Some participants leave with profound insights.
Some leave with a sense of relief.
Some leave with a completely different relationship to a pattern they have carried for years.

Whatever unfolds, you won’t be doing it alone.

You’ll be held by experienced facilitators, a small group, and a process that continues beyond the retreat itself through an online follow-up gathering focused on integration and support.

Three sisters meditation trees 

In the Dos Colinas community, we are so honored to have a section of primary rainforest where we can sit with wise, strong and beautiful trees. Most of them are over 30 years old. Their age alone is proof of their strength and resilience, but beyond that, they are also a profound source of knowledge.

Whenever we sit beside them, we feel a deep sense of calmness and belonging—as if humans were meant to listen carefully, in silence, to all their teachings.

Somehow, this is no longer a ritual that most of us get to experience. Yet it remains a beautiful opportunity for people from all over the world who visit Toji to reconnect with this feeling. In that stillness, many confusions begin to clear, and a more aligned path through life can reveal itself.

🍃 To Rest Like a Tree
“Praise and blame, gain and loss, pleasure and sorrow come and go like the wind. To be happy, rest like a great tree in the midst of them all.” - Buddha’s Little Instruction Book

This week, we gift you a simple Qi Gong practice from our @mariosqigong , for those days when you feel you need to release tension and restore energy. This practice can be done at any time, anywhere.

Daily life builds tension in the body, mind, and energy system, making it harder to maintain vitality. 
A simple way to return to stillness is to stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hips relaxed.

Let your weight rest naturally on the skeleton. 
Imagine your joints as pearls connected by a thread of awareness, creating a unified, open body. 

Allow the sacrum to lengthen downward and the crown of the head to rise gently upward. 

Relax the shoulders, arms, and hands. 
Let gravity ground you through your feet.

Stay open, relaxed, and natural, allowing energy, breath, clarity, and inner calm to flow freely.

For the past 15 years, we have been pioneers of vegetarian and vegan cuisine within the Finnish retreat scene, continuously developing our kitchen with both wellness and sustainability at heart.

At Villa Mandala, you will enjoy colorful, delicious, and nourishing vegetarian meals three times a day. Carefully selected organic and locally sourced ingredients — some from our own garden — nurture both you and the rest of life on our planet. Our beloved kitchen staff lovingly prepares you a menu that is rooted in the principles ethical eating. We want ahimsa to be present on the plate as well — without ever compromising on quality.

Villa Mandala’s menu is designed to be 

🥥 mostly gluten-free and dairy-free
🫶 low in refined white sugar
🥦 mostly organic and local
❤ as vegan as possible, with only a few exceptions in certain side dishes. 
🫛 we always incorporate some raw food elements and plenty of fresh herbs into our cuisine. 
👌The meals are rich in fiber, protein, and wholesome slow-release carbohydrates …and ahhh, so full of flavor! 

💧 The water at Villa Mandala comes from our local natural spring; drink as much as your soul desires, as water this pure and free from chemicals is rarely available. #retreatcenter #resortlife #yogavibes #visitscandinavia

🌿 Nachhaltigkeit beginnt bei den eigenen Entscheidungen.

Im Kleebauer Hof Retreat Center wird Achtsamkeit nicht nur gelehrt, sondern konsequent gelebt. Deshalb wurde die bisherige Ölheizung durch eine moderne Pelletheizung von ÖkoFEN ersetzt. Ein wichtiger Schritt für mehr Klimaschutz, regionale Wertschöpfung und eine nachhaltige Zukunft. 💚

Wo früher rund 36.000 Liter Heizöl pro Saison benötigt wurden, sorgt heute erneuerbare Wärme für Wohlfühlatmosphäre im gesamten Retreatbetrieb. Ein starkes Zeichen dafür, dass Komfort, Wirtschaftlichkeit und Umweltbewusstsein Hand in Hand gehen können. ♻️✨

Welcome back to Meet Us Monday!

Meet Osha!

Osha is our youngest female. She was born at Heartland Farm on April 23, 2018 to Ashwani and Goldylocks. She was born deaf; therefore, we have chosen not to use her for breeding. Osha was not named after the Occupational Safety and Health Administration! She is named after an herb called osha (Ligusticum porteri) that grows in the Rocky Mountains.

Osha has one blue eye and one brown eye and lovely white fleece. She is unique both in looks and in personality. She always looks a little goofy, and her personality always makes people laugh. However, don't be deceived, Osha will not back down from the bigger girls. She stands up for herself - especially if food is involved.

One interesting characteristic Osha has is that she almost always keeps a stalk or two of hay in her mouth which she often rolls with her tongue.

🌿Collaborators wanted. 🌿

Everything we do is an invitation to drop back into the body. From the wood-fired bread we bake to the linen we touch, we believe that the objects surrounding us carry a specific frequency.

As we prepare for the upcoming seasons, we are curating the physical experience of the valley. We want to fill our spaces with tools, scents, and textures that actively support integration, stillness, and deep practice.

So we are looking to partner with conscious brands that create with intention. For example:

🌿The Tools: Sustainable yoga mats and organic meditation props that hold our practices.

🌿The Rituals: Clean candles, sustainable incense, and herbal teas that soften the morning transitions.

🌿The Nourishment: Specialty coffee, adaptogenic supplements, and functional plants that ground the nervous system.

🌿The Living Space: Premium textiles and sound systems that blend seamlessly into the forest.

We don’t look at your follower count. We look at your alignment. We offer a living, breathing sanctuary where your creations are not just displayed, but truly lived by a community that values deep presence.

Let’s co-create a sanctuary. If your brand speaks this language, send us a DM or an email and let’s bring your vision to the valley.

Er is iets bijzonders aan een groep die terugkomt.

Hij liep het landgoed op met zijn koffer, keek om zich heen en zei het meteen. Dit is een heel ander gezicht, zo groen.

De laatste keer waren er kerstlichtjes. Leefden we binnen, dicht op elkaar, de winter die ons naar binnen trok. Nu staat alles open. Groen, levend, de zon die de heuvels raakt en de avonden die uitnodigen om buiten te blijven.

Dezelfde mensen. Dezelfde plek. En toch een volkomen andere ontmoeting.

Dat is wat Terra Nova doet. Het beweegt mee met de seizoenen, elk met zijn eigen gezicht, zijn eigen warmte, zijn eigen uitnodiging. En wie hier terugkomt ontdekt elke keer iets nieuws.

We zijn blij dat ze er weer zijn.

I celebrate the wealth of experiences and growth the last year has offered me. I have been humbled to the ground facing chronic pain and losing my ability to ‘do’ much at all for an extended period of time. Yet there has been so much sweetness in amongst it all and some of my biggest life lessons yet🙏🏽🙏🏽

Moving forward into 2025 I commit to continuing to listen to my body’s wisdom as I strengthen my body to be robust, agile and strong to be able to enjoy all that I love pain free.

I continue to support myself and others to discover and embody abundant inner spaciousness and equanimity. Cultivating an ability to stay present and offer the best version of myself even in the face of challenges when they arise. This year I will continue to balance the pusher with love and eradicate any unnecessary stress from my body and my life. Ultimately achieving this creates more room in my entire being for joyful, loving and blissful moments each and every day. 

I offer my passions and gifts to others effortlessly and without attachment. I magnetise people with similar intentions for personal transformation and feel lucky to have the opportunity to deeply connect with others. The results, synergies and abundance for my clients and myself continue to expand to in new and profoundly beneficial ways.

This year marks my 10 year anniversary of running retreats here in the UK and all over the world!! 
It’s never easy as there is so much involved with the creation of these retreats, but they are always so so rewarding. 

Here are the places we have been, which was your favourite?

Kidlington, Oxfordshire (2-3 weekends a year, now I hold 2 weekends a year in the Cotswolds)

Chatel, French Alps (my first overseas retreat has run yearly since, I also help other teachers create there dream retreats here)

Kerala, India (A truly memorable trip, beautiful villa right on the sea, including an amazing day trip on the back waters),

Sri-Lanka (2 retreats have been held here in 2019 and last year, I will definitely be returning, this country is just incredible)

Island of Brac, Croatia
Sardinia
Marrakech (3 times, we return in 2027)
Sabine Hills, Italy 
Sicilly

Upcoming 

Portugal (2026)
Ithaca, Greece (2027)

Only 4 Rooms left! Gift yourself a A luxury week of Ashtanga Yoga in Scotland! Join this community and breathtaking nature hosted by Katy Scherer and Joseph Armstrong. From the 19th - 26th of April 2024.

Join Joseph and Katy for a week of traditional Ashtanga Mysore practice as well as daily breathing and meditation sessions and afternoon workshops on a variety of topics related to the practice. Each day we start the morning sadhana with breathing and meditation followed by our traditional Mysore practice with both Katy and Joseph caring for the room.

The afternoon sessions will consist of a variety of topics which will be confirmed closer to the date once Katy and Joseph have a good understanding of the students joining. Topics will consist of Asana, philosophy and Breathing techniques. Mysore is an incredibly inclusive form of practice and welcomes all levels to join in the fun. Both Joseph and Katy have many year of experience teaching complete beginners to advanced students so you will be in very capable hands.

Nestled within the Cairngorms wilderness and with easy access to Aviemore, The Dell offers a main lodge and cottages offer the perfect escape for anyone looking to dive deep into their yoga practice.

The Dell has created the perfect setting to unwind, stoke your fire, re-connect and breathe deep. You can choose to go barefoot on the lawn, pick a hammock, toast your toes and slow right down all whilst taking in the silence amongst the trees.

On the retreat you can expect a very hearty brunch that will be served after our morning session and a spectacular evening dinner with a few snack on offer in between. Coffee and Tea are alway available.

It all begins with practice. Maybe you want to start from scratch. Maybe you want to reconnect to the mysore style. Or maybe you just want to go deeper, surrounded by nature and inspired by community.

All levels are welcome, complete beginner to 7th series. The spark of contentment that comes with yoga practice will be supported for all!

Don’t miss out because you are... A. Unsure if you can take time off of work. 🙈
***alert*** adults NEED to take time off. Especially if you are going through any big life shifts over the past few months to a year. We need time to process that shit and honor our need to self care, rejuvenate and restore. 
Retreats do exactly that.

B. You don’t have the money.🙊
***alert*** If you say you don’t have the money, you totally won’t. But, the truth is, when you want something really bad- both you and the universe will come up with a way to afford it. Also, we have payment plans. Annnnd, the retreat is still 7 months away.

C. You feel like a yoga beginner and everyone else is going to be “better than you”.🙉
***alert*** every single one of our retreats have someone who is brand new to yoga. That’s a good thing! Our retreats are designed for all levels and you are welcome to participate in as much or as little as you want. Yoga is not the only thing we do. I can’t believe how much I’ve learned the past couple of years. Sometimes I just sit in awe of everything I’ve been through, every obstacle I’ve overcome and how much I’ve healed my inner child ❤️‍🩹

I never thought I would be able to regulate my nervous system, grow my resilience and embody my past, present and future.

The last year was one of the hardest years. I’ve learned that spirituality can be toxic, that people who preach kindness are not always kind to the people around them, that people (especially) from the spiritual world can also be selfish and inconsiderate. 

But I’ve also learned that I can decide what I resonate with. I don’t have to agree with everyone and I can stand up for myself. I can leave situations I don’t feel welcome anymore and I can go my own way ❤️‍🔥

Embodying every little bit of myself feels so good and I am finally where I always wanted to be 🫶🏼

It’s just about to start - stay tuned 🙏🏼

We are connected to so many people online but yet so many of us feel disconnected to ourselves because THIS is what is missing in our lives.

It’s being around people where you truly feel SEEN.

It’s being in spaces where you can fully be yourself & experience environments that bring out the best in you.

The word “yoga” means unity.

And unity comes from CONNECTION. 

Connecting with the deepest parts of your soul, your purpose, and who you are the core of your being beyond all the roles you play in your daily life. 

And at the same time connecting with others who light a fire in you, open up your heart & shift your perspective and belief in yourself in ways you never expected. 

This is exactly what my retreats are about. 

They are about you breaking down so many walls & opening so many doors inside yourself…

While building relationships with other people that enrich your life in ways that you can’t ever put a price tag on.
