Hier sind unsere Top 5 Must-Sees, die du bei deinem Besuch auf keinen Fall verpassen darfst:

1️⃣ Capdepera: Historisches Grenzdorf mit einer mächtigen, mittelalterlichen Festung über dem Meer.
2️⃣ Sa Calobra: Spektakuläre Serpentinenstraße führt zu einer atemberaubenden, tiefen Sturzbach-Schlucht.
3️⃣ Es Salt des Freu: Ein versteckter, märchenhafter Wasserfall inmitten eines mystischen Tramuntana-Waldes.
4️⃣ Formentor: Wilde, windgepeitschte Halbinsel mit einem majestätischen Leuchtturm auf Klippen.
5️⃣ Strand Es Trenc: Traumhafter, naturbelassener Sandstrand mit karibisch anmutendem, kristallklarem, türkisfarbenem Wasser.

Egal, ob du durch die alten Festungsmauern wanderst oder das Karibik-Feeling am Strand genießt – Mallorca wird dich völlig neu überraschen.

📍 Must Visit în Mallorca: Sa Calobra

Unul dintre cele mai spectaculoase locuri de pe insulă, cu ape turcoaz, stânci impresionante și peisaje care îți rămân în memorie. 🌊⛰️

🛣️ Drumul până aici are 26 de serpentine spectaculoase și este considerat unul dintre cele mai frumoase drumuri panoramice din Europa.

🏞️ Lângă plajă se află canionul Torrent de Pareis, declarat monument natural.

🐐 În zonă vei întâlni frecvent capre sălbatice, considerate un simbol al Munților Tramuntana. Sunt obișnuite cu turiștii, dar nu este recomandat să le hrăniți.

🦎 În canionul Torrent de Pareis trăiește salamandra neagră de Mallorca, o specie rară și endemică insulei, care nu se găsește nicăieri altundeva în lume.

📍 Salvează această locație ca să o vizitezi când vei fi în Mallorca! 🌊⛰️

Bucket list climb up Sa Calobra 🏔️ 

#sacalobra #mallorca #spain #cycling #bike #velo #strava #mallorcacycling

I set out to break Sa Calobra in sub-30 minutes. It took three attempts, two failures, lots of caffeine, and countless lessons. Read my story (link in bio) as a great example of how failure is the path to success — it will help you achieve your next cycling goal.

Don’t be fooled by the smiles… 😅I went down Sa Calobra gripping the brakes so hard I had to stop twice because my hands hurt.

Spent the entire descent wondering why I was voluntarily cycling to the bottom of a mountain I’d then have to climb back up. The road just kept going… and going… and going.

The cheeky beer and cake at the bottom felt like heaven as I mentally prepared myself for the climb back up (while secretly hoping a tour bus might take pity on me and offer me and my bike a lift).

Somehow, I made it back to the top. Fuelled by cake, determination, questionable decision-making, and a playlist that eventually turned the climb into an outdoor spin class.

And somewhere between the suffering, the views and the cake, I think I’m starting to understand why people love this. Turns out voluntarily suffering up a mountain is weirdly rewarding 🫶🤭

WHEELTALK: I have always believed that it’s best to have a few sets of wheels for different demands — deep sections for speed on flatter courses, shallows for the mountains, and sturdy alloy wheels for winter training. I still do to a certain extent, but I am starting to believe there is such a thing as “one rim to rule them all.” — and this will be different for everyone based on their riding style, terrain, etc. This year, in my pursuit of “the one”, I am going to build up a set of @lightbicycle 36mm rims with a 21mm internal/28mm external width, custom drilled in 22h (front), 24h (rear), so that I can lace them to some Campagnolo Hyperon Ultra Two hubs — and paired with 30mm @michelinbicycle tyres and @silca_velo latex tubes — for lower rolling resistance, an excellent aerodynamic profile, and comfort. 

I have been riding 60mm Campagnolo Bora WTOs and 25mm handbuilts for the last few years, and to be honest, I struggle to control the 60mm Bora’s, they are too deep for my riding style/weight, and slow to accelerate. The 25mm’s handle brilliantly and are really comfy, but I can’t help but feel like I am throwing away speed with such a shallow profile, and they are quite narrow compared to today’s standard — when all the evidence and pro-peloton trends suggest going wider.

So, going 36mm deep, 28mm external, 30mm tyres and latex tubes is the right formula for me. They will accelerate, handle brilliantly, be comfortable, have good aerodynamics, and low weight. Oh, and 3k carbon weave for that 2006-2015 look that I can’t let go of. And yes, rim brakes. No, tubeless.

Another example of how failure is the path to success. It took three attempts for me to complete Sa Calobra in under 30mins (aka “the sub 30”):
• Attempt 1: mechanical
• Attempt 2: 30:47
• Attempt 3: 29:53

Embrace failure as part of the learning process and nothing can stop you from achieving your goals. Full debrief to come… stay tuned.

🥾⛰️ Torrent de Pareis = verjetno najlepši trail na Mallorci! 😍

Spust v kanjon je še ogrevanje. Potem pa 4 ure skakanja, plezanja in prerivanja čez ogromne skale. 🪨💪 Vmes sem dvignil drona 🚁 in posnel nekaj res brutalnih kadrov skozi eno najlepših sotesk, kar sem jih videl.

Pokrajina? Noro lepa! 😲 Visoke skalne stene, jame, gladke skale in na koncu nagrada: turkizno morje pri 🌊 Sa Calobra.

Ko sem po 4 urah prilezel do plaže, sem imel samo eno misel: »Nazaj gor? Ni šans!« 😂

🛑 Bus ne vozi.
👍 Avtoštop še vedno dela!

Najprej me pobere francoski par 🇫🇷, nato še kuhar iz Latinske Amerike 🇲🇽👨‍🍳 in njegova ekipa. Čez nekaj minut sem že v svojem avteku z zasluženo brezalkoholno pivo v roki. 🍺😎

Za zaključek še 🍦 sladoled v Sóllerju in ogled znamenite železnice 🚂. Potem pa kapitulacija od utrujenosti. 😴

Ta trail? Takoj bi ga šel še enkrat! 🔥🤩

Ce matin, échec total dans ma quête de maison. La première était superbe, mais l'accès est impossible avec un van. La deuxième, même histoire. La troisième, pareil. Trois visites, trois déceptions.

Pour ne pas rester sur cet échec, je suis quand même parti voir cette fameuse plage, l'attraction numéro un de l'île. Après 12 kilomètres de virages à travers des paysages spectaculaires, je suis finalement arrivé à destination. Au fond, la route valait presque davantage le détour que la plage elle-même.

J'ai poursuivi l'aventure en m'engageant dans le canyon. Plus j'avançais, plus le décor devenait impressionnant. Je suis allé jusqu'au point où il était raisonnable de s'arrêter, puis j'ai rebroussé chemin. Une journée qui n'a pas donné les résultats espérés, mais qui m'a tout de même offert de beaux paysages et quelques moments de découverte.

🚴‍♂️⛰️ Mallorca Adventures – Part 2 🇪🇸☀️

While Mallorca’s food and wine were unforgettable, the real magic for us was exploring this incredible island through its mountains, coastlines, and legendary cycling routes.

One of my favorite moments was riding to Cap de Formentor 🚴‍♀️ with Dave 🚵‍♀️, known by Mallorcans as the “Meeting Point of the Winds,” sits at the northernmost point of Mallorca and is famous for its dramatic cliffs, breathtaking sea views, and iconic lighthouse overlooking the Mediterranean. 🌊⛰️✨

While Dave tackled Formentor with Allan and Sheldon the day prior, I joined Robin on an epic trek through La Victòria and Talaia d’Alcúdia. What an adventure! We climbed rocky goat paths, navigated rugged terrain, and shared the trail with mountain goats while taking in incredible panoramic views. 🥾🐐⛰️. 

For Dave’s birthday 🎂, he conquered the legendary Sa Calobra climb—a bucket-list achievement for cyclists worldwide.  Watching him take on one of cycling’s most famous roads was inspiring, and he absolutely crushed it! 🎉🚴‍♂️

And after cheering him on, I took on my own challenge: climbing the 365 Calvari Steps in Pollença... twice! 💪😅 The staircase consists of 365 steps, one for every day of the year, and is lined with traditional Mallorcan stone homes. At the summit sits the beautiful 18th-century Església del Calvari (Calvary Chapel), home to a revered 14th-century wooden sculpture of Christ. The reward for reaching the top? Sweeping panoramic views over Pollença’s terracotta rooftops, lush valleys, and the distant Formentor Peninsula. 

From world-class cycling and mountain adventures to breathtaking coastlines and unforgettable views, Mallorca exceeded every expectation. We’re already dreaming about our next visit. ❤️🇪🇸

Hi, I’m Dawn!

I thought I’d take a moment to introduce myself because behind every retreat is a person, a story, and a heart behind the vision.

I’m a Reiki Master, holistic guide, and the founder of transformational retreats created for those who are ready to slow down, reconnect, and experience themselves in a deeper way.

My journey has always been about understanding people. With a background in Sociology and a Master’s degree in Human Services with a focus on counseling, I’ve spent years learning about connection, growth, healing, and the stories we carry.

But my greatest teacher has been my own transformation.

I know what it feels like to outgrow an old version of yourself and feel called into something deeper. That journey is what inspired me to create retreats that are not just vacations — they are intentional spaces to reflect, release, reconnect, and remember who you are.

My retreats blend meaningful conversations, healing practices, cultural experiences, and moments of joy because I believe transformation can be powerful and still feel beautiful.

I’m honored to guide these experiences and walk alongside you.

Sharing a very special weekend in the Austrian Alps @derboeglerhof and the Pure Nature Spa.

This family-run wellness hotel is truly exceptional and exceeded all my expectations. Child friendly, there truly is something for all ages - with a fabulous ‘adults only’ spa, entirely separate from the general one. Both are excellent.

I treated The BF as it’s his birthday this weekend - and we both LOVED the icy cold wild swim pond (they break the ice in the winter!!) and the daily scented sauna rituals too. The gym is excellent as was the morning yoga and afternoon sound baths…

The Alpbachtal is a great area in the Alps too for mountain walking, hiking, cycling and winter sports. Have you been? Do share your experiences. Only an hour from Innsbruck too.

And you’ll need the activities as the food here is amazingly good - fresh, local, seasonal and exquisitely presented by charming staff.

I’ll be putting a full review over on www.lizearlewellbeing.com wellness travel section soon, but in the meantime, a very big Thank You/Vielen Danke to the familie Duftner. I can honestly say Der Boglerhof is now one of my all-time favourites - and I can’t wait to bring more of my family to visit.

Do save this for future reference - you’ll be thanking me ❤️ LX

The Role of Tailored Culinary Experiences in Elevating Corporate Events and Retreats.

Creating a lasting sensory experience is one of the key pillars of success for specialized corporate events and wellness retreats. For Buono’s latest catering project in Toronto, we had the privilege of partnering with the Raha Bash retreat to design a bespoke culinary experience. The objective was to craft a menu that not only complemented the serene, mindfulness-focused atmosphere of the event but also delivered the highest standards of authentic, premium Italian dining.

​By understanding the unique dynamics of the retreat, we struck a precise balance between clean, refined flavors and the holistic energy of the environment. The highlight of the evening was a curated lineup of premium, miniature Italian finger foods:
​Mortadella Crostini: Paired with fresh stracciatella cheese and a rich pistachio pesto.
​Prosciutto Crudo Bruschetta: Topped with fresh wild arugula , cherry tomatoes,  stracciatella and shaved Parmigiano Reggiano.
​Caprese Bruschetta: Featuring blistered cherry tomatoes, ricotta cheese, fresh basil and a glossy balsamic glaze.
​Miniature Eggplant Parmigiana: Served in individual artisanal boats for a seamless, upscale presentation.

​To complete the wellness journey, a vibrant selection of organic, cold pressed juices accompanied the guests during their outdoor sessions, ensuring a fully integrated health-and-wellness experience.

Моя 35 рекомендация по Дюссельдорфу ✨

Это спа особенно хорошо тем, что его атмосфера, разнообразие банек, природа, церимонии, которые проходят настолько хороши, что мы совсем перестали ездить в местные спа, хотя и в Дю есть неплохие. И вот почему: 

1. Церимонии, которые раз в час проходят в разных баньках, и в этом месте есть бесплатные и платные 
Бесплатные - это не просто помазать медом, помахать веером , каждая церемония - это история в выдержаной тематике спа. Я назвала ее друидско-будистской. Не уверена, что так правильно, но ощущения именно такие! 

Платные церемонии тоже отвал какие необычные, если интересно, пишите, расскажу вам на что мы ходили)

Особенно рекомендую остаться на последний две, они там просто уходят в отрыв! Именно с этой точки еще раз хочу отметить, что персонал, который там работает - это просто какой-то восторг. 

Они не просто мило улыбаются, они там будто дзен все постигли (в самом лучшем смысле этой фразы) они такие лучезарные, что хочется просто находиться рядом, от этого уже хорошо. 

Главный плюс этого спа - есть дни, когда можно приходить в купальниках. И это находка для поездки с компанией/семьей. Не все готовы к голому спа :) 

Если честно, я побывала во многих спа в Германии, Голландии и аналогов этому пока не знаю. Всем ценителям и любителям очень рекомендую. 

Внутри есть возможность арендовать халат, полотенца и шлепки, но стоить это будет дороже входного билета на день, поэтому рекомендую купить свои 🥲

Как всегда, если было полезно - ставьте лайк и подписывайтесь 🙏🏽 в профиле @shulgaad  еще больше прикольных мест и стильных образов, заглядывай 🫂 

на регулярное ведение этой рубрики вдохновляете меня исключительно вы. Я благодарю каждого за обратную связь, которую вы даете этим роликам 🫶🏼

And I don't mean aesthetically.

I mean walking into a room with energy. Sleeping through the night. Carrying your own bags. Climbing the stairs without thinking about it. Not needing 3 coffees before noon.

The women in my family have a history of diabetes and heart disease. I watched them go through a system that managed their symptoms and never once asked why. No one looked upstream. No one asked what they were eating, how they were sleeping, whether they were moving. They were given medications for conditions that had root causes nobody addressed.

I decided a long time ago that wasn't going to be my story.

Strong women over 40 aren't superhuman. They just do a few things differently:

They lift weights at least twice a week
They eat enough protein instead of just eating less of everything
They protect their sleep like it matters — because it does
They walk daily. Not as punishment. As maintenance.
They've stopped treating rest as laziness and started treating it as strategy

This is not about fighting your age. It's about working with your body.

Perimenopause is not the beginning of the end. It's the beginning of doing things properly.

Comment STRONG and I'll send you the retreat details. 💪🌿

June is Men’s Mental Health Awareness Month 💙

This month means a lot to me personally and the birth of Rhyset. 

There was a time in my life when I was struggling. I experienced loss, faced challenges with my own mental health, and found myself searching for purpose, connection, and healing. Those experiences changed me, and ultimately led to the creation of Rhyset.

Rhyset was never just about retreats. It was created to be a safe space where people can step away from the noise of everyday life, open up without judgement, build meaningful friendships, get out of their comfort zones, strengthen both their mind and body, and begin addressing the root causes of whatever they may be going through.

Mental health struggles can often feel isolating, especially for men who are taught to keep things to themselves. But the truth is that real strength comes from being willing to talk, ask for help, and take action towards becoming the best version of yourself.

To raise awareness this month and encourage more people to prioritise their wellbeing, we’re giving one person the chance to win a Rhyset retreat of their choice.

To enter:
✅ Like this post
✅ Comment the friend you’d love to share the experience with
✅ Follow @rhyset.retreats 
✅ Share this post to your story

Let’s continue creating conversations, breaking stigmas, and reminding people that they don’t have to go through life’s challenges alone.

Your journey matters. Your mental health matters. 💙

🌿 What Happens When Women Give Themselves Permission To Pause?
✨ International Yoga Day Edition ✨

A few little snippets from our Yoga Day Trip last Sunday 🤍

And honestly... it felt a bit like being back on a school trip 😆🚌💛

There was laughter, excitement, snacks being shared, conversations flowing, and everyone eagerly waiting to get on the bus 🌿✨

We celebrated International Yoga Day a week ahead of the official day (21st June) 🧘‍♀️🤍

Some highlights from the day 🌸

🚌 A giant bus ride that made us all feel like kids again

🌊 Yoga overlooking the sea and pool
With beautiful weather all day (minus a tiny 5-minute rain shower 😄☔) But like the yogi we are we navigated the change..🧘‍♀️

🧘‍♀️ Group yoga shape challenges
Working together, balancing together, laughing together

📸 Scavenger Hunt: Yoga Edition
Teams had to recreate different yoga poses around the resort and some of the creativity was incredible 😂💛

💃 Bringing yoga to the dance floor
A little movement, a little cardio, and a lot of laughter

🏊‍♀️ Pool fun and relaxation
Because wellness should include joy too 🌿

One thing I noticed throughout the day...

As adults, making new friends can feel surprisingly difficult 🤍

Life gets busy.
Responsibilities increase.
And often our circles become smaller 🌱

That is why I love these events so much 🌸

They bring women together through a shared interest.
They create conversations.
They create connections.

And this trip was no different 💛

I watched so many new friendships begin and blossom throughout the day 🌿✨

That, more than anything, makes me grateful for this beautiful YWAY community 🤍

Thank you to every single person who joined us 🌸

More photos and videos coming soon 🎥✨

Zoey is a TCM food therapist, registered dietitian, published author of The Five Elements Cookbook, and founder of her own TCM clinic in Shanghai. Her work has been featured in the New York Times, Food and Wine, and Bon Appetit.

Tucked between lush gardens and the sacred flow of the Ayung River, this suite felt like a private sanctuary designed for deep rest and reflection. The split level layout creates a natural rhythm to the space, with a serene bedroom above and a generous living area below, both opening to calming river views. Natural wood floors, Balinese art and traditional Songket textiles bring warmth and authenticity, while the mosquito netted king bed invites truly restorative sleep.

Outside, the private pool and daybed became my favorite places to slow down, paired perfectly with the meditation pavilion for quiet moments, gentle yoga or simply being present. Evenings were especially grounding, soaking in the volcanic stone bathtub facing the garden, surrounded by nature and silence. With its sense of privacy, thoughtful design and attentive butler service, this suite is more than a place to stay. It is a space to reconnect, unwind and feel completely held by nature.

Anita arrived with a question I hear so often from high achieving women:

The truth is, the patterns that shape our lives once served a purpose. Perfectionism. People pleasing. Constant striving. They were adaptations, ways a younger version of us learned to feel safe.

This weekend, we welcome another beautiful group of guests to Italy for our June Cleanse Retreat.

As we prepare welcome packs, supplements, finalise the details, and get ready to hold space for the week ahead, we’re reflecting on what a privilege this work truly is.

Every retreat begins the same way: a group of people arriving from different walks of life.

Some arrive exhausted.
Some overwhelmed.
Some searching for answers.
Some simply knowing that something needs to change.

And then something beautiful unfolds.

New friendships are formed.
Energy begins to return.
Perspectives shift.
People reconnect with themselves in ways they never expected.

Because the transformation isn’t really about the cold pressed juice, the treatments, the workshops, or even the breathtaking Tuscan surroundings.

It’s about creating the space to pause.To listen. To reconnect. To remember who you are beneath the noise of everyday life.

As we welcome our June guests, we’re reminded that transformation often begins with one simple decision:

The decision to choose yourself.

So wherever you are today, we invite you to reflect:

• Where in my life am I being called to create more space?
• What part of myself am I ready to reconnect with?
• What would it look like to truly prioritise my wellbeing?

To our June retreat guests, we cannot wait to welcome you.

And to our wider community, thank you for walking this journey with us.

With love from Tuscany 🇮🇹✨

Tina & Jess xx

Follow @goodneuroscience to understand your brain. 
Stress isn’t just a feeling. When your brain perceives a threat, your amygdalae fire, your hypothalamus activates, and your body releases cortisol before you’ve had a chance to think.   What brain imaging research shows is that this response is changeable.   After 8-week mindfulness programs, participants show reduced amygdala reactivity and stronger connectivity between the amygdala and the prefrontal cortex, the region that regulates the alarm response. 

Cortisol reductions have also been observed across multiple studies.   These aren’t self-reported improvements. They’re measurable changes in how the brain processes and responds to threat, and they show up in scans. 
Studies:
Mindfulness Practice Leads to Increases in Regional Brain Gray Matter Density https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3004979/ 
Neural Mechanisms of Symptom Improvements in Generalized Anxiety Disorder Following Mindfulness Training https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3777795/ 
Neurobiological Changes Induced by Mindfulness and Meditation: A Systematic Review https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11591838/

I’ve started sleeping a lot and I can’t seem to stop. What needs to digest has taken over, and I’m letting it.

I’m giving myself space to rest and process. I’m honouring the urges to procrastinate, to pause, to pivot. I’m deliberately devouring books, kisses from my family and all the emotions I still somehow manage to avoid.

The thing is, I thought creating and contributing were my superpowers. But I’m beginning to wonder if my resistance to stopping has always been a trauma response.

So I’m digesting. Eons of going, of striving, of fighting. And the light feels dim right now but I know it’s not forever.

This moment is both hard and auspicious, both joyful and aggravating, both unknown and full of promise. It is entirely mine.

A woman resting as an act of rebellion.
A woman resting for her daughter’s daughters.

A woman who doesn’t know exactly where this road leads, but trusts that she knows how to walk it.
.

The greatest lesson of Gautam Buddha was not about changing the world—it was about understanding yourself.

Born as a prince, he had everything that people spend their lives chasing: wealth, comfort, power, and status. Yet he realized that none of these could protect a person from suffering, aging, illness, or death. In search of truth, he left behind a life of luxury and embarked on a journey that would inspire millions for centuries.

Buddha taught that peace does not come from controlling everything around us. It comes from mastering our own mind. Most of our suffering arises from attachment—to people, possessions, expectations, and outcomes. The more tightly we hold on, the more pain we create for ourselves.

His wisdom remains timeless in today's fast-paced world. When life becomes overwhelming, Buddha reminds us to pause. To breathe. To observe our thoughts without becoming them. To respond with compassion rather than anger, and with understanding rather than judgment.

Happiness is not found in having more. It is found in wanting less. Strength is not shown through dominance, but through self-control. And true freedom begins the moment we stop seeking validation from others and start finding contentment within ourselves.

Like a lotus flower that blooms beautifully from muddy waters, every human being has the ability to rise above difficulties and transform suffering into wisdom. The path may not always be easy, but every step taken with awareness brings us closer to inner peace.

"Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without."

#gautambuddha  #buddhism #mindfulness #InnerPeace

Why a Junk journal 
~
Because sometimes the hardest part of creating isn’t knowing what to make. It’s believing you have permission to begin.
~
A blank page can carry a surprising amount of psychological weight. For many people, it wakes up perfectionism, self-criticism, or the fear of getting it wrong before the first mark has even been made.
~
That’s why I love working with old envelopes, magazine pages, scraps of paper, and pages from well-loved books.
~
They’re already imperfect.
They already have a story.
~
From an art therapy perspective, the material matters because it changes the psychological demand placed on the individual.
~
A blank page often asks us to perform. A page with creases, words, stains, and history quietly says something different:
~
“You don’t have to create perfection here.”
“You only have to respond.”
~
Sometimes that’s enough to soften the inner critic and help the nervous system move from protection into exploration.
~
Healing isn’t always about changing yourself. Sometimes it’s about changing the conditions that allow a different part of you to emerge.
~
What kind of page feels safest for you to create on—a completely blank one, or one that already has a story?
~
Stay curious. Stay courageous. 🤍

When I moved to Arizona I thought the hardest part would be starting over in a new place but the hardest part was actually  realizing I had no idea who I was outside of what everyone expected from me.

I didn’t know what I liked, what I wanted, what I needed…Because for as long as I could remember I had been so busy being whatever everyone else needed me to be that I never stopped to ask myself those questions and it manifested itself into my relationships, my business, my marriage🥲

I’d tell Derek I needed more support and he’d ask what that looked like and I’d go blank because I genuinely had no idea. I had never allowed myself to take up space so I had no language for it.

And then I’d get frustrated at him for not understanding something I couldn’t even explain to myself😅

That’s what happens when you spend your whole life making sure everyone else is okay and forget to ever check in with yourself & the moment I started getting quiet enough to actually hear myself (to figure out what I actually needed) everything in my relationships started to shift because I finally realized what I needed..

The biggest lesson I’ve learned: People can only meet you as far as you’ve met yourself..

So If you’re in a season of unlearning and building life on your terms, follow along🫶🏻 you’re not alone here

One of the most difficult lessons in life is recognising that not everything can be fixed, changed, or controlled.

We often believe that if we worry enough, think hard enough, or keep searching for the right answer, we’ll eventually find certainty. But some situations ask something different of us.

They ask for acceptance.

Acceptance isn’t giving up.
It isn’t approval.
And it doesn’t mean you like what’s happening.

It means acknowledging reality as it is, rather than exhausting yourself fighting against what you cannot change.

There is wisdom in learning the difference between what requires action and what requires surrender.

When we stop investing our energy in battles we cannot win, we create more space for what truly matters: our relationships, our values, our wellbeing, and the present moment.

Sometimes peace begins not with changing your circumstances, but with changing your relationship to them. 🌿

This variation takes a traditional lunge and adds a few extra layers of challenge. 🔥

Placing the front foot on the short box increases the demand on stability, balance, and control through the standing leg while allowing for greater range through the hips and ankle. The straps add resistance through the upper body, encouraging connection through the back, shoulders, and core. 💪

Adding rotation challenges the body to move across multiple planes while maintaining alignment and control, improving coordination, balance, and rotational strength. 🌀

The goal isn’t simply to perform a lunge, but to maintain length through the spine, organize the pelvis, and create controlled movement from the center. 🎯

For a long time I’ve been walking an unknown path. Only able to see the next step but not the entire trail. It’s a special place to be when you feel change is pulling but you don’t know where it’s taking you. It’s not the first time I’ve been compelled towards something new so I trust the process and playfully tag along. Because sometimes what the heart already knows the mind doesn’t understand yet. I’ve made many changes in the way I live, the way I work and move through life. I’ve educated myself in yin yoga, Pilates and restorative yoga. And I’m excited about integrating it in future projects and workshops. I recently turned my old photography prop room into a little artwork studio, because I feel the longing to play with new ways of expressing myself creatively. Also, my nerdy passion for iPhone photography grows. And even though I still love my good old dslr, I shoot 99% of my photos on the phone. But most importantly, I’ve changed how I run my business. I’ve closed many doors, stopped following all the “should do’s”, the algorithm trends, and gone back to what I used to love. Writing long newsletters from the heart, sharing my images, quotes and hosting workshops for creative women. (My Soulful Glow workshop next week is sold out, but I plan on doing one more in the fall). I’ve gone from looking at my future with no idea of what to do next, to being excited to test all my new ideas. So if you struggle with change, the feeling of not knowing what direction to take; do not judge or criticize yourself. It’s absolutely okay to pause and be thoughtful about your next move. It’s okay to slow down and wait for the answer within. In fact, the more mindful stillness you invite into your life, the faster you’ll know what to do next. Support yourself with compassion and kindness because if you judge yourself or rush yourself; you cannot hear the true voice of your soul❤️ Trust the process ❤️

Happy beautiful day lovelies🤗🥰

Love, Christina

A arte sempre ocupou um lugar singular na experiência humana. Muito antes da escrita, nossos ancestrais já registravam símbolos em cavernas, modelavam figuras em argila e pintavam imagens carregadas de significado. Talvez porque certas dimensões da experiência humana não se expressem primeiro em palavras, mas em imagens.

A psicologia analítica de Carl Jung propôs que sonhos, mitos e produções artísticas podem revelar conteúdos profundos da psique. Segundo Jung, o inconsciente não se manifesta apenas por conceitos, mas também por símbolos — formas universais que atravessam culturas e épocas distintas.

Foi essa perspectiva que inspirou o trabalho pioneiro da psiquiatra brasileira Nise da Silveira.

Em um período marcado por práticas psiquiátricas invasivas, Nise seguiu outro caminho: criou ateliês de pintura e modelagem para pacientes psiquiátricos, permitindo que o mundo interno encontrasse uma forma de expressão visual. Seu objetivo não era ensinar arte, mas observar como imagens espontâneas poderiam revelar processos psíquicos em curso.

Ao longo de décadas, o trabalho desenvolvido no Museu de Imagens do Inconsciente reuniu milhares de obras que revelaram a recorrência de símbolos semelhantes aos encontrados em mitologias e tradições de diferentes culturas.

Hoje, a neurociência amplia esse diálogo ao demonstrar que a prática artística mobiliza simultaneamente sistemas ligados à atenção, emoção, memória, percepção e coordenação motora.

Entre neurônios e símbolos, a arte ocupa um lugar singular: ela é simultaneamente expressão subjetiva, processo biológico e ferramenta de investigação da mente humana.

Com o pré-lançamento do TeraPinturas, inicia-se uma jornada dedicada a explorar os encontros entre arte, psicologia e neurociência.

In a contemplative tale, it is said that the Buddha first taught metta to 500 monks who ventured into a congenial forest. 

However, the unseen forest devas residing there became fearful of the monks' presence. 

To scare them away, these beings created frightening sights and sounds, which eventually terrified the monks and disrupted their mindfulness. 

Seeking help, they approached the Buddha and shared their experiences. 

The Buddha responded with wisdom and compassion, urging the monks to return to the same forest and continue their meditation, but this time armed with the teachings of metta as their protection. 

The monks followed the Buddha's advice and went back to the forest. 

As they persisted in their practice, the fearful sights and sounds ceased to exist. 

Through calm and peaceful meditation, each monk eventually achieved enlightenment, becoming an arhat. 

With Devin Berry

Vivimos en una época donde la mediocridad se ha vuelto normal.

No porque falte talento.
El talento sobra.

Lo que escasea es disciplina, carácter y responsabilidad personal. @drstephanedecrock 

La mediocridad no siempre se ve como fracaso.
Muchas veces se disfraza de comodidad, de excusas, de distracción constante.

Es elegir lo fácil cuando sabes que podrías hacer más.
Es hablar de cambio, pero evitar el esfuerzo que el cambio exige.

Los filósofos griegos llamaban areté a la realización del máximo potencial humano.
Vivir con excelencia no era un lujo. Era un deber.

Pero la excelencia incomoda.
Exige disciplina.
Exige pensar por uno mismo.
Exige asumir responsabilidad por la propia vida.

Por eso muchos prefieren criticar a quien intenta superarse antes que enfrentar su propia pasividad.

La pregunta no es si el mundo está lleno de mediocridad.

La pregunta es otra:

¿Vas a formar parte de ella… o vas a decidir vivir por encima del promedio?

Shi Heng Yi (born in 1983) is a Shaolin Master, teacher, and spiritual guide whose teachings have inspired millions of people around the world. Through his unique blend of Shaolin philosophy, mindfulness, and practical life wisdom, he helps people cultivate inner peace, discipline, and self-awareness in a fast-paced modern world. His insights often bridge ancient Eastern teachings with contemporary challenges, making timeless wisdom accessible to a global audience.

Raised within a culture that places strong emphasis on gratitude, respect, and personal responsibility, Shi Heng Yi frequently speaks about the importance of understanding our roots and appreciating the people who have shaped our lives. His teachings encourage individuals to look beyond personal grievances and recognize the deeper connections that make life possible.

Over the years, his message has resonated with people from all walks of life because it focuses on universal principles: self-mastery, compassion, resilience, and gratitude. Rather than seeking fulfillment through external achievements alone, he encourages people to develop a stronger relationship with themselves and those around them.

In this clip, Shi Heng Yi explains that life contains powerful forces capable of pulling people in countless directions. Without a stable inner root, it is easy to become lost in emotions, trends, fears, and external influences. He encourages everyone to discover the part of themselves that remains steady beneath life's constant changes—a place that observes without being shaken. When that foundation is found, challenges may still arise, but they no longer have the power to take away your sense of self.

I asked a new bride what home feels like after six months of marriage.

She was quiet for a few seconds.

Then she said:

“Home feels like a place I miss.”

I had to put my phone down.

Because behind that one sentence was a story so many women never tell.

She wasn’t being mistreated.

She wasn’t being abused.

She wasn’t even unhappy all the time.

She was just… lonely.

She missed walking into a room where nobody judged her mood.

She missed being understood without having to explain herself.

She missed hearing her name spoken with familiarity instead of formality.

She missed being someone’s daughter before becoming everyone’s responsibility.

Every day she smiled.

Every day she adjusted.

Every day she told her parents she was doing fine.

Because that’s what good daughters do.

And that’s what good daughters-in-law are expected to do too.

But some nights, after everyone had gone to sleep, she would sit quietly and wonder:

“Why do I feel homesick when I’m already home?”

That’s the grief nobody prepares women for.

The grief of leaving one life behind before you’ve fully found your place in another.

The grief of belonging everywhere and nowhere at the same time.

And over time, if that grief remains unspoken, it can become anxiety.

It can become emotional exhaustion.

It can become a loneliness that nobody else sees.

Read that again.

Not every wound comes from cruelty.

Some wounds come from constantly missing a place, a version of yourself, and a feeling you can never fully go back to.

If this resonates with you, follow this account for mindfulness, emotional healing, laughter therapy, and gentle support for navigating life’s most difficult transitions with more peace and self-compassion.

PRAKTIK DOA MALAM 

Ya Tuhan,
malam ini aku pulang ke dalam diriku sendiri.

Aku lepaskan perlahan semua yang hari ini tidak perlu aku bawa sampai tidur:
energi yang bukan milikku, kata-kata yang sempat melukai, pikiran yang terlalu ramai,
dan kekhawatiran yang belum tentu benar-benar terjadi.

Terima kasih, Tuhan.
Bukan karena hari ini semuanya mudah, tapi karena aku masih diberi napas,
masih diberi kesadaran, masih diberi kesempatan untuk memilih jalan yang lebih baik.

Rawat hatiku malam ini.
Bukan agar aku tidak pernah sakit,
tapi agar rasa sakit tidak mengubahku menjadi pahit.

Tuntun aku agar menjadi manusia yang lembut.
Yang bisa mencintai tanpa kehilangan diri.
Yang bisa memberi tanpa mengosongkan jiwa.
Yang bisa bertumbuh tanpa terus menghukum diri sendirI

Dan Jagalah orang-orang yang aku sayangi.
Dan jagalah juga aku dari bagian diriku yang sering terlalu keras, terlalu menuntut sempurna, terlalu memaksa kuat, sampai lupa bahwa aku juga butuh istirahat.

Malam ini, aku izinkan tubuhku tenang.
Aku izinkan pikiranku berhenti berlari.
Aku izinkan hatiku percaya, bahwa tidak semua hal harus selesai hari ini.

Besok, bangunkanlah aku dengan ruang yang lebih lapang. Pikiran yang lebih jernih. Hati yang lebih ringan. Dan kesadaran bahwa hidupku sedang bergerak,
meski belum semua jalannya terlihat jelas.

Aku berserah, Tuhan.
Bukan karena aku menyerah, tapi karena aku percaya
ada kekuatan yang lebih besar
sedang menuntunku, menjagaku,
dan merapikan hal-hal yang belum mampu aku pahami.

Amiin.

📌Скільки болю потрібно людині, щоб їй нарешті дозволили сказати: МЕНІ ВАЖКО?

Тобі боляче? Завжди знайдеться той, кому болючіше
Тобі страшно? Завжди знайдеться той, кому страшніше
Тобі важко? Завжди знайдеться той, кому важче.

І так до безкінечності, а от питання а схералі ви таке собі придумали? 

Ми живемо у дивному світі, де людина постійно змушена доводити право на власний біль. Наче існує якась невидима комісія, яка вирішує, чи достатньо ти постраждав, щоб отримати співчуття. Де ви бл@ть, комісія? 

Але правда в тому, що для тих, хто знецінює, завжди буде малооооооо! Бо справа ніколи не була у вашому болю. Справа у їхній нездатності його витримати. І найстрашніше, що дуже часто люди починають вірити у чужий біль лише тоді, коли вже нічого не можна змінити. Коли людини більше немає поруч, коли вже не потрібно слухати, коли вже не потрібно підтримувати, коли вже запізно.

Тому, можливо, варто перестати шукати тих, кому гірше. І просто навчитися іноді говорити: Я не знаю, наскільки тобі боляче, але я бачу, що тобі боляче🙏🏻 Як я можу тобі допомогти? 

Бо людяність починається саме там, не в порівняннях, а в здатності побачити людину❤️

മനസ്സിനും ശരീരത്തിനും ഒരുപോലെ ആശ്വാസം നൽകാൻ ശ്വസന വ്യായാമങ്ങൾ (Breathing Exercises) സഹായിക്കും. അമിതമായ ഉത്കണ്ഠയും മാനസിക സമ്മർദ്ദവും (Tension & Stress) കുറയ്ക്കാൻ ലളിതമായ ഈ ശ്വസനരീതി ശീലമാക്കാം:
 ആഴത്തിലുള്ള ശ്വാസമെടുക്കൽ (Deep Breathing): പതുക്കെ മൂക്കിലൂടെ ശ്വാസം ഉള്ളിലേക്ക് എടുക്കുക. വയർ നിറയുന്നത് പോലെ തോന്നണം. കുറച്ചു സെക്കൻഡ് ശ്വാസം അടക്കിപ്പിടിച്ച ശേഷം പതുക്കെ വായിലൂടെ പുറത്തേക്ക് വിടുക.
 4-7-8 രീതി: 4 സെക്കൻഡ് കൊണ്ട് ശ്വാസം ഉള്ളിലേക്ക് എടുക്കുക, 7 സെക്കൻഡ് അത് അടക്കിപ്പിടിക്കുക, പിന്നീട് 8 സെക്കൻഡ് എടുത്ത് പതുക്കെ പുറത്തുവിടുക. ഇത് മനസ്സിനെ പെട്ടെന്ന് ശാന്തമാക്കും.
ദിവസവും കുറച്ചു മിനിറ്റുകൾ ഇതിനായി മാറ്റിവെക്കുന്നത് വഴി മാനസികാവസ്ഥ മെച്ചപ്പെടുത്താനും സമാധാനം കണ്ടെത്താനും സാധിക്കും.

⬇️ Non servono stravolgimenti per sentirti meglio. Bastano piccole abitudini quotidiane, fatte con costanza, per cambiare davvero il modo in cui ti senti ogni giorno 🌿

Dieci minuti di respiro consapevole resettano il sistema nervoso e fanno allontanare l’ansia da sola.

Dormire almeno 7 ore permette al cervello di fissare i ricordi della giornata e di rigenerarsi davvero.

Usare il telefono per troppe ore aumenta il rischio di sentirsi giù, anche se non ce ne accorgiamo.

Camminare 30 minuti al giorno produce nel cervello gli stessi effetti di un antidepressivo lieve.

Scrivere quello che hai in testa per 15 minuti fa scendere il livello di stress senza nemmeno sforzarti.

Il benessere non è complicato. È fatto di piccole scelte ripetute ogni giorno 🤍

📌 Salva questo carosello e prova ad applicarne anche solo una oggi 🌸

A veces llamamos imperfecciones a cosas que en realidad son parte de nuestra esencia. Esa que nos caracteriza. 

Como esa forma tan tuya de reírte, tus manías, lo sensible que eres, lo mucho que sientes o las cosas que te hacen diferente no son defectos.
Simplemente no todo el mundo sabrá entenderlas.
Por eso no tienes que esconder tus rarezas ni cambiar para gustarle a nadie. Solo tienes que elegir bien a quién le das acceso a esa parte tan auténtica de ti.

Las personas correctas no verán imperfecciones. Verán justamente aquello que te hace especial. ❤️

¿Le tienes miedo al vacío? 👁️🧘‍♂️

Vivimos bombardeados de pensamientos, corriendo de una idea a otra sin detenernos. 

Pero, ¿qué pasa cuando empezamos a autoobservarnos a través de la meditación y la respiración consciente?

Al oxigenar nuestro cerebro, esta increíble “máquina cuántica”, empezamos a descubrir algo fascinante: el espacio que existe entre un pensamiento y otro.

Ahí, en esa pausa, es donde ocurre la verdadera reconfiguración. Al aprender a habitar ese espacio, hacemos las paces con la “nada” y con el vacío; un lugar que a muchos les genera ansiedad o miedo a dejar de existir, pero que en realidad contiene la paz más pura.

No se trata de no pensar; se trata de simplemente ser y estar. ✨

#Meditación #PazInterior #Mindfulness #Autoobservación #Conciencia

Sekarang, luangkan waktu sejenak untuk membuka hati dan mengizinkan diri menerima lebih banyak kebaikan dalam hidup.

- Saya mengizinkan diri saya menerima rezeki yang berkah dari arah yang tidak disangka-sangka, melalui cara-cara yang baik, halal, dan membawa manfaat.

- Saya mengizinkan diri saya menerima peluang, dukungan, dan keberlimpahan yang terus bertumbuh setiap hari.

- Saya mengizinkan diri saya menerima kemudahan dalam memenuhi setiap kebutuhan dan kewajiban hidup saya.

- Saya percaya bahwa selalu ada jalan, pertolongan, dan solusi yang sedang bergerak menuju hidup saya.

- Saya terbuka untuk menerima keajaiban, kemudahan, dan keberlimpahan yang selaras dengan kebaikan tertinggi bagi diri saya.

Tarik napas perlahan…

Ucapkan dengan penuh keyakinan.

Lalu izinkan dirimu percaya bahwa hal-hal baik juga bisa terjadi untukmu. ✨

Tape transfer 
~
What if the most important part of an art activity isn’t the finished piece… but what the material asks of you along the way?
~
In art therapy, we don’t just pay attention to the image that’s created. We’re also curious about the relationship between the individual and the material.
~
This tape transfer technique is a great example. It asks you to make decisions, trust a process you can’t fully control, tolerate uncertainty, and stay present while the outcome is still unknown.
~
These aren’t just creative skills.
They’re psychological ones.
~
Every time we practise staying with uncertainty in a manageable way, we gently expand our capacity to cope with uncertainty beyond the page.
~
That’s why art materials can become such powerful therapeutic tools. They don’t just help us express ourselves—they help us notice ourselves.
~
And often, that’s where change begins. If you’re curious about the psychology behind creativity, art therapy, and the nervous system, follow along. I’ll keep sharing simple creative processes that reveal what’s really happening beneath the surface.
~
💭 What did you notice in yourself while watching this process?

Recenzja „Uważność małej żabki” autorstwa Eline Snel, od wydawnictwa @wydawnictwo_cojanato 

✨dla dzieci w wieku 18 msc-4 lat (moim zdaniem książka jest dla rodziców dzieci 1-3, a nagrania medytacji 3-6)
✨dla rodziców (nagrania z książki również dla specjalistów) 

Seria „żabek” była mi niezmiernie polecana z różnych źródeł - tiktok, specjaliści. Po przeczytaniu nie rozumiałam dlaczego, aż włączyłam nagrania.

Książka, którą tu widzicie jest pierwszą książką z serii żabkowej. Ma nam ona przedstawić medytację dla najmłodszych pociech. Pozycja jednak jest spisem doświadczeń autorki, innych rodziców i rad dotyczących rodzicielstwa. Jest też bazą o tym jak wychować dziecko i wprowadzić uważność oraz medytację do codziennego życia 

Moim zdaniem sama książka to krótkie wyjaśnienie podstawowych „zasad” rodzicielstwa dla osób, które nie czytały raczej książek o rodzicielstwie. Myślę, że to, co tu się pojawia powinno być bazą wiedzy każdego rodzica.

Książka porusza temat tego, jak istotne jest stawianie granic dziecku jednocześnie przy zrozumieniu dla jego emocji. Jest o tym, jak ekrany szkodzą. O tym, że dziecko uczy się poprzez modelowanie (naśladowanie rodzica). O tym, że nie trzeba dziecku angażować 100% czasu. Jest też trochę rad dla rodziców - zatrzymaj się zanim nakrzyczysz na dziecko, słuchaj swojego ciała itd. Jeśli któryś z tych punktów jest dla Ciebie nowy - polecam kupić tę książkę. Szybko się czyta :)

Dla mnie jako specjalisty, całe piękno tej książki jest zawarte w jej przyjemnych do słuchania relaksacjach: poprzez kod QR z książki dostajemy dostęp do relaksacji ok. 10 minutowej dla rodzica i kilka ok. 3 minutowych relaksacji dla dzieci. 

Książka moim zdaniem jest zbędna dla psychologa, ale same nagrania zdecydowanie są warte uwagi. Dla rodzica - jak wyżej, to fajna podstawa wiedzy o rodzicielstwie wraz z nutką relaksacji i uważności :)

Boredom has become something we rush to escape.

A few seconds of waiting? We reach for our phones.
A quiet moment? We fill it with noise.
An empty afternoon? We distract ourselves.

But what if boredom isn't a problem to solve?

Psychologists suggest that boredom serves an important purpose. It signals that your mind is craving meaning, novelty, challenge, or growth. In fact, moments of boredom activate the brain's "default mode network"—a state linked to creativity, self-reflection, future planning, and problem-solving.

Many of our best ideas don't arrive when we're busy.
They arrive when we're staring out of a window, taking a walk, sitting quietly, or simply allowing our minds to wander.

Boredom is not wasted time.
It's mental space.

The next time you feel bored, resist the urge to immediately scroll. Pause for a moment and ask yourself:

✨ What is this feeling trying to tell me?
✨ What am I avoiding?
✨ What am I ready to explore next?

Sometimes, boredom isn't the end of engagement.
It's the beginning of discovery.

By acknowledging the stress and reframing your internal narrative, you can harness your body’s physiological response to boost performance, increase focus, and build resilience. 

Are you going to let stress build you up or break you down? You get to decide!

Куда бы ты ни поехал,

свои мысли ты берёшь с собой.

RESET.

Bai Ze is a mythical beast of wisdom from ancient Chinese legend.

It is said that Bai Ze knew the names of spirits, monsters, and unseen beings. But his power was never about violence.

Bai Ze’s gift was clarity.

When life feels covered in mist, Bai Ze does not force the road open. He does not choose for you. He stays near, quietly helping the hidden things take shape.

A fear becomes something you can name.
A shadow becomes something you can face.
A lost path becomes a direction.

Bai Ze reminds us that awakening is not always loud.

Sometimes, wisdom arrives softly.

Like a light behind the fog.
Like a gaze that sees through confusion.
Like a companion who helps you recognize yourself.

Not to save you from the journey,
but to help you see it clearly.

Pasamos de armar un prode con amigos a llevar un casino abierto las 24 horas en el bolsillo, y lo naturalizamos como si fuera pasión deportiva.

Las plataformas de apuestas deportivas no te venden "entretenimiento"; usan diseño conductual puro para hackear tu sistema de recompensas. Disfrazan el azar de "conocimiento futbolístico" para que creas que tenés el control, pero el único objetivo es que tu cerebro se vuelva adicto al refuerzo intermitente.

La ludopatía moderna es silenciosa y ocurre en el sillón de tu casa. ¿Cuáles son las banderas rojas a las que familiares y amigos deben prestar atención?

- Incapacidad de disfrute: Si ya no podés sentarte a mirar un partido sin meter plata, el deporte dejó de importarte. Tu cerebro solo busca el pico de dopamina de la apuesta.

- El celular blindado: Irritabilidad extrema si te interrumpen mirando la pantalla, ocultamiento de billeteras virtuales y aislamiento físico estando en la misma habitación.

- La ilusión del experto: Justificar las pérdidas hablando de "cuotas" o "estrategias", creyendo que es una inversión y no un juego de azar diseñado para que la banca gane.

No es pasión. Es un negocio que parasita tu atención y tu tranquilidad. 

Si el partido solo tiene sentido si hay plata de por medio, el juego ya te empezó a jugar a vos.

Are you busy living... or busy avoiding? 

Humans are incredibly creative.
We can find a thousand ways to distract ourselves from the one person we can't escape: ourselves.

No judgment. We all have our favorite hiding spots. 😉

A little scrolling, a little working, a little fixing everyone else's problems... and suddenly we forgot to ask:
"How am I really doing?"

The funny thing is, the moment we stop running, we often discover we had been avoiding living our own lives. We feel empty, sad... but that's the turning point: that's where we begin the real work: take control of our choices and our reality.

Sometimes all it takes is one breath to come back home. 🌱

Which distraction has your name on it? 👇

I’ve been labelled an overthinker for as long as I can remember, and I feel like more or less everyone has. But there is a difference between overthinking and reflection.

True reflection isn’t just letting your mind race; it’s an active, mindful process of metacognition (thinking about our own thoughts). When we step back to assess *why* we are thinking a certain way and what drives those narratives, we stop fighting the thoughts and start listening to the emotions underneath.

By understanding the root feelings fuelling our inner dialogues, we can finally begin to process them rather than just getting lost in the noise. 

And the next time someone says you’re an overthinker, you can take it as a compliment 🧠✨

#Psychotherapy #Metacognition #overthinking #Mindfulness #MentalHealthMatters

“Se você está em um lugar e sua cabeça está em outro, você não está em lugar nenhum.” Essa citação do pai da atenção plena, Jon Kabat-Zinn, traduz perfeitamente a mensagem que eu quero passar para você hoje. 

Vivemos em modo multitarefa quase o tempo todo. A mente raramente está onde o corpo está, e esse distanciamento constante tem um custo alto: mais ansiedade, menos clareza e uma sensação permanente de que algo está em atraso.

Quando conseguimos habitar o agora com lucidez e gratidão, a mente desacelera, dando vazão a melhores ideias, decisões mais conscientes e uma relação mais saudável com tudo ao redor.

Para que isso aconteça, é preciso encontrar uma atividade que te permita se desconectar. No meu caso, essa ferramenta é a corrida. Por mais irônico que possa parecer, é justamente o momento que a minha mente para de correr atrás de tudo e me permite ver as situações com mais nitidez.

Não existe uma ferramenta universal. O que importa é que ela te traga de volta para o agora e que haja constância e intenção.

Você tem uma ferramenta que te reconecta com o presente? 

#Mindfulness #Desconexão #Autocuidado #Corrida

Piernas arriba de la pared (Viparita Karani) 🌿

Una de las posturas más simples y restaurativas del yoga. Al elevar las piernas, el cuerpo recibe una pausa profunda que favorece el descanso y la recuperación.

✨ Beneficios principales:
• Ayuda a aliviar la sensación de piernas cansadas o pesadas.
• Favorece el retorno venoso y la circulación.
• Relaja el sistema nervioso y reduce el estrés.
• Puede disminuir la hinchazón de pies y tobillos.
• Libera tensión en la zona lumbar.
• Calma la mente y prepara el cuerpo para un sueño reparador.
• Invita a un estado de introspección y quietud.

A veces, el verdadero bienestar no llega haciendo más, sino permitiéndonos detenernos. Con las piernas elevadas y la respiración suave, el cuerpo recuerda cómo descansar y la mente encuentra un refugio de calma. 🕊️✨🧘‍♀️

Set Your Intention
When decisions become difficult or distractions arise, let your intention guide your next step. An intention helps you move from reaction to direction. It becomes a guiding light that keeps you aligned with who you want to be, regardless of what circumstances arise.

Examples:

✨ "I intend to respond with patience."
✨ "I intend to prioritize my health."
✨ "I intend to lead with kindness."
✨ "I intend to stay focused on what matters most."

Write your intention somewhere visible a journal, phone wallpaper, sticky note, or planner. Throughout the week, return to it often.

#IntentionChallenge #Mindfulness #PersonalGrowth #IntentionalLiving #SelfAwareness #GrowthMindset #TheTimeIsNow #Befitment

Mindfulness

Most people move through life reacting out of habit, conditioning, and emotion without ever stopping to ask why. Mindfulness interrupts that cycle. It awakens us and gives us the opportunity to pause, reflect, and choose our response rather than simply reacting.

Every choice we make, no matter how small, has an impact on the direction of our lives. When we make decisions with awareness, we take ownership of the outcomes. We may not always get the result we hoped for, but we can find peace knowing we acted consciously and with intention.

Living mindfully doesn’t mean being perfect. It means being awake enough to learn from our experiences, adjust our course when necessary, and continue growing. The more conscious we become, the less we are controlled by fear, impulse, and old patterns. We begin to live deliberately rather than accidentally.

Awareness is the foundation of personal growth. It is where wisdom is born, where transformation begins, and where true freedom is found.

One of the more affirming connections with depth psychology, contemplative traditions, Indigenous wisdom, and leadership development is around the teaching that avoidance does not make something disappear.

It simply moves it out of awareness.

Carl Jung suggested that what remains unconscious is often experienced as fate...

Buddhist traditions speak of habitual patterns that continue shaping perception until they are brought into awareness...

Indigenous teachings often remind us of the importance of right relationship - with ourselves, with one another, and with the larger living world...

To turn toward what we would rather avoid. 

Or as Rumi often expressed poetically with this paradox... what appears to be a all-consuming fire is often the source of divine relief, and that the tests we dread are exactly what cool and transform the soul...

The grief we postpone.
The fear beneath our aspirations.
The uncertainty beneath our certainty.
The difficult conversations.
The parts in us we would rather not claim.

Recent work in cognitive science and posthumanist thought expands this conversation even further. A fave thinker and professor of mine Dr. Katherine Hayles suggests in her evolutionary work that much of cognition operates beyond conscious awareness...

Long before we “decide” something, countless processes are already sensing, interpreting, anticipating, and responding...

In other words, what shapes our lives is often far larger than what we consciously notice.

Whether in organizations, communities, relationships, or our own inner lives, transformation rarely begins with having all the answers.

It begins with the willingness to look honestly at what is already there. Not with judgment, or with shame. But with curiosity, courage, and compassion.

Often the doorway to growth is hidden inside the very thing we least want to face.

PSICOTERAPIA EN TIEMPOS POSMODERNOS
Modelos y prácticas para la salud mental
Marcelo R.  Ceberio / Ana Preisz (Comp.)

El pensamiento posmoderno se caracteriza por la superación de las perspectivas dicotómicas y dualistas, abriendo paso a un horizonte plural, diverso y complejo. Impulsa el cuestionamiento de los discursos cerrados y de los textos carentes de evidencia, al tiempo que introduce un giro lingüístico decisivo: el lenguaje no solo expresa el pensamiento, sino que lo configura; no hay pensamiento sin lenguaje y, en consecuencia, el lenguaje construye la realidad. Desde esta perspectiva, la verdad deja de concebirse como un enunciado universal para pensarse como una construcción situada, una perspectiva entre otras, habilitando el reconocimiento de múltiples formas de saber que coexisten y dialogan.

M. Ceberio y A Preisz, como autores y compiladores, reúnen en esta obra a un conjunto de profesionales que inscriben su producción en el marco de a psicoterapia en el contexto de la posmodernidad. Cada apartado revisa, con rigor científico, los desarrollos actuales de la psicoterapia. El libro sitúa así en la proximidad del conocimiento vivo, aquel que se revisa, se corrige y se expande, favoreciendo el crecimiento y la evolución del campo de la salud.

Esta obra concibe la psicoterapia como una práctica ética, reflexiva y profundamente comprometida con el sufrimiento humano.  A lo largo de sus capítulos, se ponen en diálogo diversos modelos teóricos que expresan una psicología posmoderna en constante transformación, capaz de interrogarse a sí misma y de responder a los desafíos de una realidad social compleja, cambiante y muchas veces incierta. Se enfatiza la necesidad de supervisión clínica, la terapia personal y la formación continua del terapeuta como pilares indispensables de una práctica responsable.

Many people think mindfulness requires special training, a therapy session, or long meditation practices. In reality, mindfulness can be as simple as taking a slow breath, paying attention to your surroundings, listening deeply to someone you care about, or fully enjoying a meal.

Mindfulness is about bringing your attention to the present moment—without judgment. It’s a skill you can practice anywhere, anytime, and often in just a few minutes.

Small mindful moments can make a big difference in how we cope with stress, regulate emotions, and reconnect with ourselves.

✨ You don’t need perfection. You only need presence.

The Mary Magdalene Oracle is about a vision of radical love that formed long before Christianity became a formal religion, long before Mary’s gospel was excluded, destroyed and buried. It’s a vision of a beloved community that risked their lives to recognize each other as equals – innately worthy of love, no matter their status within the Roman Empire. And it’s about a vision that Christ gave Mary to give to us, then and now – to see less with the ego and more with the eye of the heart.

In this stunning oracle, Harvard-trained feminist theologian Meggan Watterson leads us through Mary’s lost teachings to integrate the profound love from her gospel into a daily spiritual practice.

Sunday Design Crush. By @michaelkendrickarchitects Shaped by a low-impact, material-driven design approach, this Scandi-inspired cabin is hidden away from view, blending seamlessly into the protected woodland.
 
The client’s vision for this cabin was to enhance the ecological biodiversity of the area, protect wildlife and enable guests to understand and appreciate the fauna, flora, and unique history and nature of the area.
 
The subtle yet refined design is modest in scale and uses the sloping site, where the lodge appears elevated amongst the trees as the ground levels fall below. Large picture windows on both the front and rear façade offer the cabin a sense of distinct transparency, enabling visitors to benefit from stunning natural views from all lodge areas and fully immerse themselves in the setting.
 
Throughout the year, the lodge is flooded with natural light, while self-tinting electrochromic glass grants privacy and limits overheating and the spill of artificial light as dusk falls. Critically, using this glass also ensures that the lodge does not disrupt the site’s bat activity, helping to secure the long-term habitat of this protected species.
 
Within, a simple yet welcoming layout includes an open-plan living space with a log-burning stove. This uses timber sourced from fallen trees on-site to heat the lodge in winter - significantly lowering the lodge’s running costs and creating a comfortable, Scandi-inspired retreat.
 
Inspiration from around the world.
Located in Fairlight, East Sussex

Our *BIG* news!

🌞It’s been a tough secret to keep, but we’re ready to announce the opening of our guest retreat in Santa Fe, New Mexico - @casa.chicoma!🌿🐶

Made for earth & dog lovers, Casa Chicoma is a collection of five eco-friendly guest casitas, surrounded by a regenerated desert oasis — gardens, fruit trees, greenhouses, a natural swimming hole, and many outdoor living spaces, along with hiking trails into the hills beyond. 

Last September, we visited Santa Fe and were overwhelmed by the abundance of culture and dog-friendliness of the city. It truly felt like a tiny Austin in New Mexico, without the brutal summer heat. We had the idea to create a dog-friendly home away from home for guests to come and enjoy this unique city, it’s abundance of nature and beautiful weather. 

Though our property is only 12 minutes from the Santa Fe plaza and super close to some *amazing* food, it feels a world away from the city out here under the stars. The 2.5 acre space features a cold plunge swimming hole, a geodesic dome “fig house”, a greenhouse supporting olive trees & grapevines, veggie gardens, an outdoor community dining table, views of the Sangre de Cristo mountains, and our five separate casitas which can be reserved individually or together as a group retreat. 

We know not everyone wants to travel right now, but we hope you’ll follow along @casa.chicoma and put us in your back pocket for the future! AND on Friday, we will be doing a two-night stay (up to $350 value) giveaway, so stay tuned for that!

We appreciate any support in getting this dream off the ground and we hope to host you soon! :)

✨ Where Time Stands Still ❄️
Winter brings a quiet magic to the Exodus Property. The world slows down, and each morning feels like a fresh, still breath. This is our season of peace, where the crisp valley air invites you to step outside and find your natural rhythm.

🌅 A Symphony of Mist and River
Nothing beats stepping into the wilderness before the world wakes up. Watch the heavy mist roll into the valley, tracing the Nymboida River as golden sunlight breaks through the trees. We feel incredibly blessed to care for this land and love sharing its magic. It is the perfect place to clear your mind and just breathe.

🧠 Winter Light Wellness
Getting outdoors early provides a powerful health boost. Science shows that morning light pauses your sleep hormones, giving you instant energy and helping you rest deeper at night. That fresh air also boosts serotonin to naturally lift your mood.

🌲 Awaken Your Senses
Our wild surroundings invite us to embrace the brisk weather, leave distractions behind, and find true warmth outdoors.

📘 ⚖️ CHUYÊN MỤC: CƯ DÂN & PHÁP LÝ ECO RETREAT 

Xin chào Quý Anh/Chị chủ sở hữu và cư dân tương lai của Eco Retreat

Trong quá trình sở hữu một bất động sản, chúng ta thường gặp rất nhiều vấn đề liên quan đến hồ sơ, thủ tục, quyền lợi, nghĩa vụ và các công việc cần thực hiện ở từng giai đoạn khác nhau.

Từ khi ký các thoả thuận ban đầu, hợp đồng, thanh toán, nhận nhà, hoàn thiện nội thất, đăng ký cư dân, khai thác cho thuê, quản lý tài sản cho đến các thủ tục pháp lý phát sinh trong quá trình sử dụng.

Nhằm tạo một kênh tham khảo và trao đổi thông tin hữu ích cho cộng đồng, Group chính thức triển khai chuyên mục:

📘 CƯ DÂN & PHÁP LÝ ECO RETREAT

Chuyên mục sẽ chia sẻ các nội dung liên quan đến:

✅ Hồ sơ và thủ tục dành cho cư dân

✅ Quyền lợi và nghĩa vụ của chủ sở hữu

✅ Các hướng dẫn thực tế trong quá trình sử dụng bất động sản

✅ Kinh nghiệm quản lý và lưu trữ hồ sơ

✅ Các câu hỏi thường gặp từ cư dân

✅ Những vấn đề pháp lý, hành chính và vận hành liên quan đến cuộc sống tại khu đô thị

✅ Những vấn đề pháp lý, hành chính liên quan ban quản lý vận hành, ban quản trị chung cư, ban tự quản khu dân cư thấp tầng ảnh hưởng đến mỗi cư dân và gia đình tại khu đô thị.

TRƯỚC MẮT:
Trong giai đoạn hiện nay, khi Eco Retreat đang chuẩn bị bước vào thời kỳ bàn giao nhà, loạt bài đầu tiên của chuyên mục sẽ tập trung vào các chủ đề:

🏡 Chuẩn bị nhận nhà

📄 Hồ sơ và thủ tục liên quan đến cư dân

🔨 Các vấn đề thường gặp khi hoàn thiện căn nhà

📋 Quản lý hồ sơ và tài sản sau nhận bàn giao

Các nội dung được chia sẻ dưới góc độ tham khảo, kinh nghiệm thực tế và hướng dẫn thực hiện, giúp cư dân có thêm thông tin để chuẩn bị tốt hơn cho quá trình sở hữu, sử dụng và quản lý bất động sản tại Eco Retreat.

👉 Nhận nhà lần đầu: Cần chuẩn bị những gì?

Welcome to this majestic 16th-century farmhouse, a place where history, nature, and opportunity come together in perfect harmony. Set within the tranquil landscapes of the Anoia region, this extraordinary estate spans 50 hectares of forest and farmland, offering panoramic views, privacy, and an authentic connection to the Catalan countryside.

Just 55 minutes from Barcelona, this historic masia, first documented in 1522, stands proudly on a 507,100 m² estate (125 acres). The main house, in good condition, offers 810 m² of built space across three floors plus additional annex buildings.

The ground floor welcomes you with a grand entrance hall leading to a series of inviting rooms: a cozy living room with fireplace, a separate dining room also with fireplace, a music room, an office, a games room, guest toilet, kitchen, laundry, workshop, and access to the woodshed.

On the first floor, eight double bedrooms and two bathrooms are arranged around a spacious central landing, while the top floor offers a central room with four adjoining spaces, a washroom, and storage rooms under the traditional sloped roof.

Beyond the main house, a caretaker’s residence and annex buildings — around 311 m² in total — await restoration, along with several historic ruins of old dwellings, barns, and storerooms.

The surrounding land, alive with Mediterranean flora and wildlife, offers endless possibilities — from creating a private family retreat to developing a rural hotel, eco-farm, or agrotourism project. 

This remarkable masia embodies the essence of Catalan heritage — serene, majestic, and steeped in history — inviting you to shape its next chapter amid the timeless beauty of the Catalonian hills.

@massalagrosecoresort, Spain’s first fully eco-conscious hideaway just outside Barcelona. It’s not your usual resort, everything here is designed with real intention.

From the moment you arrive, you notice the care in every detail, natural materials like wood, clay, and linen everywhere, no synthetic products in sight. The architecture blends seamlessly with the surrounding forest and vineyards, making it feel like part of the land, not on top of it.

What really stood out to me was how the place encourages you to slow down. No cars buzzing around, no noise pollution, just peaceful mornings with herbal teas and long walks through the hills. It’s that kind of calm that stays with you.

And the food! Organic, seasonal, and locally sourced whenever possible - even earned a Michelin Green Star for their sustainable approach. Every meal felt like a celebration of real flavors, nothing rushed or overdone.

The highlight for me was the @aireancientbaths_es, an immersive experience inspired by ancient traditions, with thermal pools lit only by candlelight, designed to reset your nervous system. It’s not just a spa, it’s a space to disconnect completely.

A quiet, thoughtful escape that leaves you feeling refreshed and more connected to nature and yourself 🌱

@sadhranabagh  sits quietly on the edge of Gurugram — close enough to feel the city’s hum, but far enough to forget it.

It’s a thoughtfully designed retreat by Pradeep Sachdeva — just 4 villas built with mud walls, old timber, and carved stone. Around them: marigold fields, wide skies, and a silence broken only by birdsong.

Each house feels different. One has a verandah wrapped in bamboo. Another opens out to the crops. There’s a library if you want to disappear for a while, and a kitchen where things are cooked slow, seasonal, and simple.

You might spend your mornings watching peacocks walk through the garden. Or afternoons just sitting — with a book, or with nothing at all.

Comment "gurugram" for details

Top experiences include:

🧘 Finding stillness with yoga and meditation in quiet, peaceful garden spaces
🔭 Joining guided birdwatching tours to the nearby Sultanpur National Park
🔥 Enjoying outdoor dining with a barbecue by the pool or set within the garden
🏛️ Appreciating sustainable architecture built with mud walls, old timber, and carved stone
🦚 Observing peacocks wandering through the grounds as you enjoy the wide open skies

When you stay at our Tropical Casita at Wilder Sanctuary, your experience is entirely up to you.
Choose a simple stay surrounded by nature, or opt for a stay with all meals included and enjoy nourishing, garden-inspired cuisine prepared with love.
If you decide to explore the wonderful local cafés and restaurants nearby, we’ll happily share our curated list of favorite spots. And no matter which option you choose, you’ll still enjoy complimentary morning treats delivered to your door each day.
Throughout your stay, you’ll have the opportunity to taste Wilder — from fresh fruits ripened on our trees to medicinal herbal teas, handcrafted herbal elixirs, and seasonal gifts from our gardens.
We believe that connecting with nature is about more than what you see—it’s also about what you taste, smell, and experience.
We can’t wait to welcome you and share the flavors of Wilder Sanctuary with you.

Brooklyn and Bradley stayed for three nights in our Tropical Casita, and we loved preparing their morning treats and sharing the flavors of Wilder Sanctuary with them. They had such a wonderful time exploring the sanctuary, relaxing in nature, and enjoying the peaceful atmosphere that they told us they really didn’t want to leave when it was time to go.
Thank you, Brooklyn and Bradley, for spending part of your Costa Rica adventure with us. We hope to welcome you back to Wilder Sanctuary one day soon.

Glamping isn't just a trend. It’s a shift toward something grounded.

In a £3 billion industry, site owners are ditching generic setups for spaces that tell a story. Sioux-style tipis are leading the charge. These aren't just tents: they are heritage structures that breathe. 

Built by hand. Made to last. No fluff.

From a business perspective, the logic is sound. A handcrafted tipi offers a sensory experience a standard cabin can’t match: the ritual of a central fire, a circular sanctuary, and the rhythmic sound of wind on heavy canvas. It’s these details that drive bookings and keep guests coming back.

Why they work for UK sites:
- 100% biodegradable with minimal land impact.
- Naturally climate-controlled for year-round comfort.
- A unique aesthetic that stands out in a crowded market.
- Hand-stitched in Cornwall using traditional methods.

Whether it's a wellness retreat or a wedding venue, a tipi changes the energy of the land. It feels intentional. 

If you’re ready to move away from the mass-produced and invest in something properly made, let’s talk through the options for your site.

For the past four years, we’ve been quietly caring for this piece of rainforest we now call home.
When we first arrived, part of the land was former pasture covered in grass and ferns. Today, that space has become a thriving food forest and medicinal garden, while the vast majority of the land remains protected rainforest.
Monkeys move through the canopy. Parrots and toucans visit daily. Native plants flourish. Water from our natural spring nourishes life throughout the sanctuary.
We recently shared more of this journey on our new Stewardship page — the story of how we care for the land, why conservation guides every decision we make, and how every stay and retreat helps support this ongoing work.
Less than 15% of the land is used for our structures, gardens, and pathways. The rest is left for nature to thrive.
Thank you for being part of this journey with us.

*Eco Retreat chuẩn bị bàn giao những căn nhà đầu tiên, kiến tạo nhịp sống xanh, bền vững*

Sau 15 tháng khởi công, đại đô thị Eco Retreat đang bước vào giai đoạn chuyển mình đặc biệt: Chuẩn bị bàn giao những căn nhà đầu tiên, chứng kiến sự xuất hiện của một nhịp sống mới, một cộng đồng và chuẩn sống mới lấy trị liệu, phục hồi, tái tạo sức khỏe con người làm trung tâm gắn với phát triển bền vững.

Giữa bối cảnh nhiều khu đô thị được định hình bởi bê tông và mật độ xây dựng dày đặc, Eco Retreat được phát triển như một "liều vitamin xanh" dành cho cư dân đô thị. Trên quỹ đất rộng 220ha, hàng triệu cây xanh, hệ thống mặt nước và những không gian chăm sóc sức khỏe đang từng bước tạo nên một môi trường sống nơi con người được trở về với nhịp điệu tự nhiên của cơ thể và thiên nhiên.

Song hành cùng sức sống đang hình thành là tiến độ triển khai ấn tượng của dự án. Theo kế hoạch, từ giữa tháng 6/2026, Eco Retreat sẽ bắt đầu bàn giao những căn nhà Home Retreat đầu tiên thuộc phân khu Mùa Hạ, đánh dấu cột mốc quan trọng trên hành trình hiện thực hóa mô hình thành phố trị liệu, phục hồi, tái tạo đầu tiên tại Việt Nam.

Khác với những ngôi nhà truyền thống, Home Retreat được phát triển như một hệ sinh thái chăm sóc sức khỏe thu nhỏ với thiết kế ưu tiên ánh sáng tự nhiên, thông gió chéo, kết nối tối đa với cảnh quan xanh đến khả năng tiếp cận tiện ích.

Home Retreat nói riêng và Eco Retreat nói chung là dòng sản phẩm phản ánh rõ triết lý mà nhà sáng lập Ecopark theo đuổi trong suốt hơn hai thập kỷ: Bất động sản không chỉ tạo ra nơi ở mà còn kiến tạo môi trường sống giúp con người khỏe mạnh hơn về thể chất, tinh thần và cảm xúc.

Pumpkin seed tofu may not be as common as traditional tofu, but it’s a beautiful way to add more whole-food nourishment to your plate. 🌱

Made from simple ingredients, this plant-based recipe is rich in protein, minerals, and nourishing fats that support the body from the inside out.

Golden on the outside. Tender on the inside. Deep, earthy flavor in every bite.

INGREDIENTS

• 2 cups raw pumpkin seeds (soaked 4–6 hours)
• 3–4 cups filtered water
• 1 tsp sea salt
• Fresh herbs (rosemary, thyme, or sage)
• Quality olive oil for searing

METHOD

Drain and rinse the soaked pumpkin seeds.

Blend with fresh water until smooth and creamy.

Strain through a cheesecloth or nut milk bag.

Heat the pumpkin seed milk over medium heat, stirring continuously.

Allow it to gently separate into curds and liquid.

Transfer the curds into a lined mold or cloth.

Press, chill, and allow it to set.

Slice and sear with olive oil, sea salt, and fresh herbs until golden.

MACROS (per serving, 4 servings)

🔥 Calories: ~350 kcal
💪 Protein: ~18g
🥑 Fat: ~30g
🌾 Carbs: ~6g (Net ~3g)

A simple, nourishing way to fuel your body with the power of plants. 🌿

Love yourself. Nourish your body. Trust your intuition. 💚

On this day of Galungan, living and celebrating with my Balinese family, I began to understand that this is not merely a festival or an event.

It is a way of life.

A daily devotion to harmony between God, Nature, and Self—a principle I have observed not only today during my first visit to a Balinese temple, but throughout the weeks leading up to this celebration.

The ceremonies reflected the communal nature of this highly evolved and integrated culture, where presence, interconnectedness, abundance, and sharing are not ideals to aspire to, but realities woven into everyday life.

I felt the same spirit while visiting family homes across Bali.

As I immerse myself in Bali, and allow Bali to immerse itself in me, I witness the subtle victory of Dharma over Adharma within and without.

This has helped me understand a profound truth embodied by these beautiful people—a living blend of the wisdom of Advaita Vedanta from an era when India flourished, the compassion of Mahayana Buddhism, and the deep animistic reverence of the indigenous Agama Bali tradition.

The lesson is simple:

The giving hand is never empty.

If we are truly one, we do not need to perform oneness.

We simply need to remove the obstacles that prevent us from experiencing it.

To become present to our naturalness.

To our interconnectedness.

To a life of sacred sharing.

To a life where every moment becomes prayful.

As I sit here with a room full of fruits, gifts, smiles, kindness, and blessings received from the people around me, I feel immense gratitude for the quiet magic of this island.

A magic that seems to render every soul who comes here a little more aware, a little more harmonious, and a little more whole.

Rahajeng Galungan. 🙏🌺
